toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_smoke ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_smoke)

# Section 9: validates the narrowed QEMU-virt RAM region + mmap composition.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_offset tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_offset PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_offset ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_offset)

vsim_add_test(test_state_loader tests/hybrid/state_loader.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_loader PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_loader)

vsim_add_test(test_abstract_command tests/hybrid/abstract_command.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_abstract_command)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_driver tests/hybrid/resume_driver.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_driver PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_driver)

vsim_add_test(test_insn_match tests/hybrid/insn_match.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_insn_match PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_insn_match)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_from_qemu tests/hybrid/resume_from_qemu.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_from_qemu PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_from_qemu)

vsim_add_test(test_memory_map tests/hybrid/memory_map.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_memory_map)

vsim_add_test(test_csr_trigger tests/hybrid/csr_trigger.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_csr_trigger PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_csr_trigger)

vsim_add_test(test_state_drain tests/hybrid/state_drain.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_drain PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_drain)

vsim_add_test(test_gdbstub_client tests/hybrid/gdbstub_client.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_gdbstub_client)

vsim_add_test(test_handoff_controller tests/hybrid/handoff_controller.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_handoff_controller PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_controller)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_file_io:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
    endif()
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

The 16x16x16 GEMM kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim and takes
~45-50s before the csrwi 0x7C0,1 drain marker fires. Combined with
the QEMU 2 spawn + gdbstub register restore (~5-10s), total e2e
runtime lands at ~55s -- right at the 60s default and prone to
spurious SIGTERMs under load. Bump only this fixture; the lighter
rt_c_hello/printf/file_io fixtures stay at the 60s default.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - Continued from prior compacted session that ported C-level e2e test cases from `/local/nick/vsim` into `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim` (cherry-picked 3 commits: a80b34a, 19894b9, 061229e; parent bump 83fb82c).
   - Explicit task this session: "investigate the local qemu build hang" — diagnose why `build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64` flakily hangs on rt_c_matmul while `/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64` (same git rev 749a874eb6) was reported reliable.
   - "go ahead" — apply the proposed fix: parameterize the inner timeout in rt_shared_mem.sh and bump rt_c_matmul's timeout to 120s in HybridConfig.cmake + run_e2e.sh.
   - "yes commit them" — split commits per Tidy First (structural vs behavioral).

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - 3-phase hybrid round-trip: QEMU 1 (TCG plugin drain) -> vsim (cycle-accurate Verilator-driven RISC-V sim) -> QEMU 2 (gdbstub register restore + run)
   - Phase switching via `csrwi 0x7C0, uimm` (CSR alias `milmb`): uimm=0 = QEMU1->vsim drain marker, uimm=1 = vsim->QEMU2 drain marker
   - TCG plugin (`libhybrid_handoff.so`) pattern-matches the csrwi via insn_match.h; serializes register/memory state to outfile
   - vsim consumes state via `--resume-from-qemu`, runs until vsim drain marker, writes `--handoff-out`, spawns QEMU 2 with `-gdb tcp::PORT -S`, connects to gdbstub, restores 217 registers, sends "continue"
   - QEMU 2 runs with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`; `_exit` uses RISC-V semihosting SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED (slli/ebreak/srai magic triple)
   - Tier-2 memory survival: `-object memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=/dev/shm/...,size=128MB` shared across all 3 processes; matrices at fixed 0x80100000+ above ELF (~0x8000d800) and below stack (0x80700000) so ELF loader's zero-fill leaves them intact
   - sim_control MMIO at 0xE0080000 (EXIT_COMMAND) / 0xE0080004 (HTIF_PUTCHAR) — only valid during vsim phase
   - rt_semihost_writes (SYS_WRITE0) — only valid in QEMU 2 phase (vsim has no semihosting)
   - `-cpu andes-ax45mp` gates Andes CSRs (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl, mxstatus, uitb, jvt, mdcause) touched by the demo crt0/handler runtime
   - TDD/Tidy First: separate structural from behavioral commits with `structural:` / `behavioral:` prefix

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_matmul.c` (read)
     - 16x16x16 GEMM split across all 3 phases. Critical for understanding the hang.
     - Phase 1 (QEMU 1): `init_matrices()` then `rt_phase_qemu1_drain()`
     - Phase 2 (vsim): `setvbuf(stdout,NULL,_IONBF,0); printf(...); matmul_kernel(); printf(...); rt_phase_vsim_drain();`
     - Phase 3 (QEMU 2): `rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_matmul: C[16][16]=\n"); for(i){ semihost_print_row(...); } verify(); _exit`
     - Matrices at fixed addresses: A=0x80100000, B=0x80101000, C=0x80102000

   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/rt_c_helpers.h` (read)
     - Drain markers:
       ```c
       static inline void rt_phase_qemu1_drain(void) { __asm__ __volatile__("csrwi 0x7C0, 0"); }
       static inline void rt_phase_vsim_drain(void)  { __asm__ __volatile__("csrwi 0x7C0, 1"); }
       ```
     - `rt_semihost_writes` uses RISC-V semihosting SYS_WRITE0 (a0=0x04) with the `slli x0,x0,0x1f / ebreak / srai x0,x0,0x7` magic triple

   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/libgloss.c` (read)
     - `_write` (fd<=2): byte-by-byte poke to DISPLAY_BASE_ADDR=0xE0080004 (vsim sim_control HTIF_PUTCHAR)
     - `_write` (fd>2): SYSCALL_3(7, fd, ptr, len) -> frontend_syscall HTIF path
     - `_exit`: SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED (a0=0x20) semihosting with semihost_block[0]=0x20026 (ADP_Stopped_ApplicationExit), semihost_block[1]=exit_status

   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh` (read + EDITED)
     - The Tier-2 driver. Parameterized: `FIXTURE`, `EXPECT_RC`, plus newly added `TIMEOUT`.
     - QEMU 1 invocation with `-cpu andes-ax45mp`, `-object memory-backend-file,...`, `-machine virt,memory-backend=mem`, `-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=$STATE_QEMU`
     - vsim invocation with `--resume-from-qemu`, `--handoff-out`, `--shared-mem-path`, `--shared-mem-size`, `--qemu-binary`
     - EDITS:
       ```diff
       FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
       EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
       +TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
       ...
       -timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
       +timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
       ```

   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` (read + EDITED)
     - VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES list drives the foreach that registers ctest cases.
     - EDITS — extended fixture format from `fname:erc` to `fname:erc[:timeout_s]`:
       ```cmake
       # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
       # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
       # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
       set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
         "rt_shared_mem:0"
         "rt_c_hello:0"
         "rt_c_printf:0"
         "rt_c_file_io:0"
         "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
       )
       foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
         string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
         list(GET parts 0 fname)
         list(GET parts 1 erc)
         list(LENGTH parts nparts)
         set(timeout_env "")
         if(nparts GREATER 2)
           list(GET parts 2 tsec)
           set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
         endif()
         add_test(
           NAME test_e2e_${fname}
           COMMAND bash -c
             "${timeout_env}FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
         )
         ...
       endforeach()
       ```

   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/run_e2e.sh` (read + EDITED)
     - Workspace harness that bypasses cmake/ctest. Hardcoded REQUIRED_ELFS and TESTS arrays.
     - EDITS:
       ```diff
       REQUIRED_ELFS=(
         handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
         rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
       + rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul
       )
       ...
         "rt_shared_mem|bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
       + "rt_c_hello|FIXTURE=rt_c_hello EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
       + "rt_c_printf|FIXTURE=rt_c_printf EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
       + "rt_c_file_io|FIXTURE=rt_c_file_io EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
       + "rt_c_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120 bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
         "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
       ```

   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` (read)
     - `RT_C_FIXTURES := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul`
     - Toolchain: `/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc`
     - `RT_C_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles -Iruntime -Wall`
     - C fixtures linked with shared runtime: crt0.S + trap.S + libgloss.c + handler.c + rt_c.ld

   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/build_qemu.sh` (read)
     - Configure: `--target-list=riscv64-softmmu --enable-plugins --disable-werror --disable-libnfs`
     - Build: `make CFLAGS="-Wno-int-conversion -Wno-error=redundant-decls" -j64`

   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/build_vsim.sh` (read)
     - podman-based: `podman build --target env -t vsim-env:latest .` then `podman run -v $PWD:/work -v $ROOT/include:/include:ro -w /work localhost/vsim-env:latest ./build.sh`

4. Errors and fixes:
   - I initially batch-ran 5 reproductions with `tail -5` then `echo "rc=$?"` — `tail` always returns 0, so I captured tail's exit code instead of timeout's. Output showed false "rc=0" five times. Fixed by removing `tail` and capturing `timeout`'s exit code directly: 8/8 rc=124 confirmed deterministic hang.
   - Compacted summary claimed `/local/nick/qemu_v5` "deterministically PASS" — actually now also hangs at the 60s inner-timeout boundary. The "deterministic pass" was just luck on prior runs.
   - Initial diagnosis assumption: hang was in QEMU 2 gdbstub register restore (per compact summary). Process snapshots disproved: at t=5,15,30,45 only vsim was alive (no QEMU 2 yet), at t=50,55 both alive. vsim spent ~45s in the matmul kernel before spawning QEMU 2.
   - `cd hybrid_vsim && git ...` failed because cwd was inside the submodule already. Fixed using `git -C /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim`.
   - To split structural/behavioral commits in a single cmake hunk, temporarily reverted the `rt_c_matmul:0:120` line back to `rt_c_matmul:0`, committed the structural parser extension, then re-edited and committed the behavioral bump.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Diagnosed root cause: rt_c_matmul's vsim phase (16x16x16 GEMM = 4096 mulw ops cycle-accurate) takes ~45-50s; combined with QEMU 2 spawn + gdbstub restore (~5-10s), total e2e is ~55s, right at the harness's hardcoded `timeout 60` boundary. System load tips it over. Not a QEMU build issue at all — both QEMU binaries (local + qemu_v5) hit the same boundary.
   - QEMU build configs are equivalent: optimization=2, debug=true, plugins=true, sanitize=none; only `werror` differs (false vs true). meson logs nearly identical except LTO/AVX/sanitizer flags all match.
   - Other rt_c_* fixtures (hello/printf/file_io) are 100x lighter and pass deterministically — confirms vsim's matmul is the heavy phase, not a generic resume issue.
   - Fix: parameterized `TIMEOUT` env in rt_shared_mem.sh (default 60), extended cmake fixture format to `fname:erc[:timeout_s]`, set rt_c_matmul to 120s. Verified: 5/5 matmul runs pass; full e2e suite: 18/18 pass.

6. All user messages:
   - Compact command: "investigate the local qemu build hang"
   - "go ahead" (apply the proposed fix)
   - "yes commit them"

7. Pending Tasks:

   - Submodule `aad0bf9` (behavioral): "bump rt_c_matmul Tier-2 timeout to 120s" — single line change tagging `rt_c_matmul:0:120` in HybridConfig.cmake. Commit message explains: "16x16x16 GEMM kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim and takes ~45-50s before the csrwi 0x7C0,1 drain marker fires. Combined with the QEMU 2 spawn + gdbstub register restore (~5-10s), total e2e runtime lands at ~55s — right at the 60s default and prone to spurious SIGTERMs under load."
   - Parent `8c55409` (structural): "bump hybrid_vsim and mirror Tier-2 timeout in run_e2e.sh" — bumped submodule pointer and added rt_c_hello/printf/file_io/matmul to REQUIRED_ELFS and TESTS arrays in run_e2e.sh, with `TIMEOUT=120` for matmul.
   
   Verification before commit: 5/5 matmul reps with TIMEOUT=120 passed; full `bash run_e2e.sh` shows "summary: 18 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped".
   
   Final assistant message: "Committed. Three commits total: hybrid_vsim a255981 (structural)... hybrid_vsim aad0bf9 (behavioral)... parent 8c55409 (structural)..."

file.content:
# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_smoke ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_smoke)

# Section 9: validates the narrowed QEMU-virt RAM region + mmap composition.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_offset tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_offset PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_offset ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_offset)

vsim_add_test(test_state_loader tests/hybrid/state_loader.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_loader PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_loader)

vsim_add_test(test_abstract_command tests/hybrid/abstract_command.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_abstract_command)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_driver tests/hybrid/resume_driver.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_driver PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_driver)

vsim_add_test(test_insn_match tests/hybrid/insn_match.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_insn_match PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_insn_match)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_from_qemu tests/hybrid/resume_from_qemu.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_from_qemu PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_from_qemu)

vsim_add_test(test_memory_map tests/hybrid/memory_map.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_memory_map)

vsim_add_test(test_csr_trigger tests/hybrid/csr_trigger.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_csr_trigger PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_csr_trigger)

vsim_add_test(test_state_drain tests/hybrid/state_drain.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_drain PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_drain)

vsim_add_test(test_gdbstub_client tests/hybrid/gdbstub_client.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_gdbstub_client)

vsim_add_test(test_handoff_controller tests/hybrid/handoff_controller.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_handoff_controller PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_controller)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_file_io:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
    endif()
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
# artifacts in this workspace.
#
# Env overrides:
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
fi

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require() {
  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
    fail "$1 not found: $2"
    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
    exit 1
  fi
}
require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"

export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN

REQUIRED_ELFS=(
  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
  rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul
)
need_build=0
for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
done
if (( need_build )); then
  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
fi

# name|command. Roundtrip fixtures share roundtrip_e2e.sh; negative drivers
# each have their own harness. Mirrors hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake.
TESTS=(
  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_shared_mem|bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_hello|FIXTURE=rt_c_hello EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_printf|FIXTURE=rt_c_printf EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_file_io|FIXTURE=rt_c_file_io EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120 bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/rt_pty_handback.sh"
)

FILTER="${FILTER:-}"
pass=0; failed=0; skipped=0
declare -a FAILED_NAMES=()
for entry in "${TESTS[@]}"; do
  name="${entry%%|*}"
  cmd="${entry#*|}"
  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
  step "[$name]"
  set +e
  bash -c "$cmd"
  rc=$?
  set -e
  case $rc in
    0)  ok   "$name PASS"; pass=$((pass+1)) ;;
    77) warn "$name (rc=77)"; skipped=$((skipped+1)) ;;
    *)  fail "$name (rc=$rc)"; failed=$((failed+1)); FAILED_NAMES+=("$name") ;;
  esac
done

echo
step "summary: $pass passed, $failed failed, $skipped skipped"
if (( failed > 0 )); then
  fail "failures: ${FAILED_NAMES[*]}"
  exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, rt_c_file_io)
# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
# -cpu andes-ax45mp gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl, ...)
# touched by the vsim-demo crt0/handler runtime. Default rv64 illegal-traps
# on the very first mcache_ctl write. The asm fixtures (rt_shared_mem.S etc)
# don't use those CSRs but still run cleanly under the Andes CPU.
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine virt,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mp \
  -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# Tier-1 C-level fixtures share runtime/ (crt0+trap+libgloss+handler) and
# runtime/rt_c.ld. Newlib is linked (no -nostdlib) for printf/fopen/...
RT_C_FIXTURES := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul
RUNTIME_DIR   := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS  := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                 $(RUNTIME_DIR)/libgloss.c $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
RT_C_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                 -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (so newlib is pulled in). The shared
# runtime is rebuilt per-fixture, which is fine -- they're tiny and isolating
# each ELF avoids stale-object pitfalls. crt0.S is passed FIRST so _start
# lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld preserves input order within
# the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

message.content: how do e2e test swtich in between?

content.text:
C-level fixtures switch phases via two `csrwi 0x7C0, uimm` markers in `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h`:

- `rt_phase_qemu1_drain()` -> `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` ends Phase 1
- `rt_phase_vsim_drain()`  -> `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` ends Phase 2

**Phase 1 (QEMU 1, TCG plugin):** runs init code with no I/O. The `libhybrid_handoff.so` plugin (loaded via `-plugin ...,outfile=$STATE_QEMU`) pattern-matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`, serializes 217 regs + the drain marker PC into `$STATE_QEMU`, and exits QEMU 1.

**Phase 2 (vsim, cycle-accurate):** `rt_shared_mem.sh` invokes `$VSIM_BIN --resume-from-qemu $STATE_QEMU --shared-mem-path $SHARED_MEM ...`. vsim's resume_driver loads regs from the state file and resumes execution at the saved PC (right after the csrwi-0). I/O works here because `_write` (libgloss.c) pokes the sim_control HTIF_PUTCHAR MMIO at 0xE0080004. vsim runs until it hits `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`, then drains its own state to `$STATE_VSIM` and spawns QEMU 2 with `-gdb tcp::PORT -S`.

**Phase 3 (QEMU 2, gdbstub restore):** vsim's handoff_controller connects to QEMU 2's gdbstub, restores all 217 regs + PC, then sends `continue`. QEMU 2 resumes at the post-csrwi-1 PC. I/O switches to RISC-V semihosting (`rt_semihost_writes` -> `slli/ebreak/srai` magic triple, SYS_WRITE0 = 0x04) because QEMU 2 has no sim_control MMIO. `_exit` uses SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED.

**Memory survival:** all three processes mmap the same `/dev/shm/vsim-rt-shared.XXX` via `-object memory-backend-file,share=on`. Fixture data (e.g. matmul's matrices at 0x80100000+) sits above the ELF's PT_LOAD end (~0x8000d800) and below the stack (0x80700000), so neither QEMU's `-kernel` load nor vsim's elf_loader zero-fill touches it.

So the switch is: code-driven CSR write -> plugin/sim observes it -> state file/gdbstub bridge -> next process resumes at the same PC, with shared mmap preserving RAM.

113:## 4. Design Decisions (locked)
192:### Phase 4 - Kanata-loop integration

180	**Goal**: vsim detects exit-detail magic CSR write, drains state, hands back to QEMU on the same shared RAM.
181	
182	New files:
183	- `src/hybrid/csr_trigger.hpp` - `CsrTrigger` class. ELF pre-scan computes the set of PCs containing `csrw 0x7C0, *`. Subscribes to retire signals (`wb_i0_pc`/`wb_i0_retire` from `src/signals/common_signals_45.hpp`). On retire-of-PC in the set, decode the saved insn to get `rs1` register, read GPR via existing probe (`gpr_index_hart0`/`gpr_value_hart0` in `src/cpu_wrappers/ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem.hpp:91-92`). If value=1, signal handoff.
184	- `src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` - dual of `qemu_resume.hpp`: halt CPU, read all archregs via DM Abstract Commands, populate ABI struct, msync to file.
185	
186	Files modified:
187	- `src/simulator.hpp:289-340` (the `run()` loop) - add `csr_trigger.tick(cpu)` after the per-cycle checks. On drain signal, break loop with new exit code `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU = 200`.
188	- `src/main.cpp` - on `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU` plus `--qemu-binary` set, after `sim.cpu.final()` (line 342), `execve` QEMU with `-object memory-backend-file,share=on,mem-path=<shared-mem-path>` and `-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,resume_from=<state.bin>`. The plugin's `resume_from` mode pokes state back via `qemu_plugin_write_register()` at the first TB callback.
189	
190	Test: extend Phase 2 program with `csrwi 0x7C0, 1; ecall` after the body. Run vsim with `--resume-from-qemu state_in.bin --qemu-binary /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64`. Verify the QEMU side processes the ecall and returns 0.
191	
192	### Phase 4 - Kanata-loop integration
193	
194	**Goal**: existing `--kanata-loop-start <pc> --kanata-loop-end <pc>` flags drive hybrid mode automatically. **No magic CSR required in the binary.**
195	
196	New files:
197	- `hybrid/scripts/vsim-hybrid` (Python orchestrator):
198	  1. Inspect ELF + user's loop PCs.
199	  2. Launch QEMU with hybrid plugin in **PC-trigger mode** (`enter_pc=<start>`, `exit_pc=<end>` plugin args).
200	  3. On QEMU exit, launch vsim in resume mode with state file + the kanata flags unchanged.
201	  4. On vsim's exit-detail, optionally re-launch QEMU to finish.
202	
203	Files modified:
204	- `hybrid/qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` - PC-trigger mode added; cheap with `qemu_plugin_register_vcpu_insn_exec_cb()`.
205	- `src/hybrid/csr_trigger.hpp` - sibling `PcTrigger` class for `--hybrid-enter-pc` / `--hybrid-exit-pc` CLI flags.
206	- `src/main.cpp` - `--hybrid-mode=auto` aliases `--kanata-loop-start/end` to the hybrid PC triggers.
207	
208	Test: boot a benchmark from `external/ax45mpv_premium/testbench/`, hand off at the steady-state inner loop, record kanata for N iterations in vsim, hand back to QEMU. The recorded kanata file for the captured iterations must be **byte-identical** to a fully-vsim baseline run for the same window (after `kanata_loop_skip` warmup).
209	
210	### Phase 5 - Hardening
211	
212	Each item independently scoped:
213	- **Vector regfile transfer**: measure DM cost; if >100ms per handoff, add mmap-shared V regfile via DPI (mirror the `set_ilm()` pattern in `src/cpu_wrappers/ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem.hpp:393-441`).
214	- **FP regs and `mstatus.FS`**: standard handling.
215	- **PMP**: all 64 pmpaddr/pmpcfg via Abstract Commands.
216	- **Priv-mode transitions**: set DCSR.prv so `dret` resumes in the right mode.
217	- **Traps inside ROI**: trap stays in vsim until exit-CSR retires.
218	- **MMIO touch in ROI**: add an interconnect watch (`src/platform/interconnect.hpp`), fail loudly on non-RAM addresses during the ROI window.
219	- **mcycle/minstret discontinuity**: sum across handoffs to keep monotonic; document the gap vs. fully-RTL.
220	- **lr/sc reservations**: explicitly invalidate via DCSR after resume.
221	- **Multi-hart**: ABI struct already carries per-hart arrays; `_NDS_NHART <= 2` already enforced (`src/cpu_wrappers/ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem.hpp:37-39`).
222	
223	Test: torture workload running interrupt-driven code, V+FP active, traps inside ROI, verifying mcycle/minstret monotonic across multiple handoffs.

file.content:
**Goal**: vsim detects exit-detail magic CSR write, drains state, hands back to QEMU on the same shared RAM.

New files:
- `src/hybrid/csr_trigger.hpp` - `CsrTrigger` class. ELF pre-scan computes the set of PCs containing `csrw 0x7C0, *`. Subscribes to retire signals (`wb_i0_pc`/`wb_i0_retire` from `src/signals/common_signals_45.hpp`). On retire-of-PC in the set, decode the saved insn to get `rs1` register, read GPR via existing probe (`gpr_index_hart0`/`gpr_value_hart0` in `src/cpu_wrappers/ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem.hpp:91-92`). If value=1, signal handoff.
- `src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` - dual of `qemu_resume.hpp`: halt CPU, read all archregs via DM Abstract Commands, populate ABI struct, msync to file.

Files modified:
- `src/simulator.hpp:289-340` (the `run()` loop) - add `csr_trigger.tick(cpu)` after the per-cycle checks. On drain signal, break loop with new exit code `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU = 200`.
- `src/main.cpp` - on `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU` plus `--qemu-binary` set, after `sim.cpu.final()` (line 342), `execve` QEMU with `-object memory-backend-file,share=on,mem-path=<shared-mem-path>` and `-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,resume_from=<state.bin>`. The plugin's `resume_from` mode pokes state back via `qemu_plugin_write_register()` at the first TB callback.

Test: extend Phase 2 program with `csrwi 0x7C0, 1; ecall` after the body. Run vsim with `--resume-from-qemu state_in.bin --qemu-binary /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64`. Verify the QEMU side processes the ecall and returns 0.

### Phase 4 - Kanata-loop integration

**Goal**: existing `--kanata-loop-start <pc> --kanata-loop-end <pc>` flags drive hybrid mode automatically. **No magic CSR required in the binary.**

New files:
- `hybrid/scripts/vsim-hybrid` (Python orchestrator):
  1. Inspect ELF + user's loop PCs.
  2. Launch QEMU with hybrid plugin in **PC-trigger mode** (`enter_pc=<start>`, `exit_pc=<end>` plugin args).
  3. On QEMU exit, launch vsim in resume mode with state file + the kanata flags unchanged.
  4. On vsim's exit-detail, optionally re-launch QEMU to finish.

Files modified:
- `hybrid/qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` - PC-trigger mode added; cheap with `qemu_plugin_register_vcpu_insn_exec_cb()`.
- `src/hybrid/csr_trigger.hpp` - sibling `PcTrigger` class for `--hybrid-enter-pc` / `--hybrid-exit-pc` CLI flags.
- `src/main.cpp` - `--hybrid-mode=auto` aliases `--kanata-loop-start/end` to the hybrid PC triggers.

Test: boot a benchmark from `external/ax45mpv_premium/testbench/`, hand off at the steady-state inner loop, record kanata for N iterations in vsim, hand back to QEMU. The recorded kanata file for the captured iterations must be **byte-identical** to a fully-vsim baseline run for the same window (after `kanata_loop_skip` warmup).

### Phase 5 - Hardening

Each item independently scoped:
- **Vector regfile transfer**: measure DM cost; if >100ms per handoff, add mmap-shared V regfile via DPI (mirror the `set_ilm()` pattern in `src/cpu_wrappers/ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem.hpp:393-441`).
- **FP regs and `mstatus.FS`**: standard handling.
- **PMP**: all 64 pmpaddr/pmpcfg via Abstract Commands.
- **Priv-mode transitions**: set DCSR.prv so `dret` resumes in the right mode.
- **Traps inside ROI**: trap stays in vsim until exit-CSR retires.
- **MMIO touch in ROI**: add an interconnect watch (`src/platform/interconnect.hpp`), fail loudly on non-RAM addresses during the ROI window.
- **mcycle/minstret discontinuity**: sum across handoffs to keep monotonic; document the gap vs. fully-RTL.
- **lr/sc reservations**: explicitly invalidate via DCSR after resume.
- **Multi-hart**: ABI struct already carries per-hart arrays; `_NDS_NHART <= 2` already enforced (`src/cpu_wrappers/ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem.hpp:37-39`).

Test: torture workload running interrupt-driven code, V+FP active, traps inside ROI, verifying mcycle/minstret monotonic across multiple handoffs.

## 8. Phase 3 E2E Test Expansion

### 8.1 Context

Phase 3 landed the round-trip mechanism. The existing e2e harness `tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh` exercises the full QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 path but with a single fixture (`handoff_roundtrip.S`) that only validates ONE GPR (`a2 == 3`) via the semihosting exit code. The plumbing works, but two large coverage gaps remain before we move on to Phase 4:

- **Register coverage**: 1 of 31 GPRs is observed; ABI-special regs (`sp`/`gp`/`tp`/`ra`/`fp`) are never touched; sign-bit and full-64-bit values are not exercised; the `HYBRID_HANDOFF_RUNTIME` trigger form (`csrrw rd,0x7C0,rs1`) is never fired in e2e.
- **Memory coverage**: zero tests today. Vsim never executes a load/store between the entry and exit triggers, so the integration of the cache/memory subsystem with the resumed state is unverified. Cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap is also untested at e2e level (Phase 1 isolated unit test only).

### 8.2 What's actually transferable today (constraints on fixture design)

From `state_drain.hpp:28-45`, `resume_driver.hpp:46-68`, `qemu_handback.hpp:122-144`, `gdbstub_client.hpp:66-82`, and `hybrid_handoff.c:101-124`:

| State class | Fully round-tripped? |
|---|---|
| `x1..x31`, `pc` | yes |
| `priv` | hardcoded to 3 in both directions |
| `f0..f31`, all CSRs (mstatus/mepc/mtvec/...), V regs, PMP | NOT transferred (Phase 5) |
| Memory written in vsim, read in QEMU 2 | NOT transferred unless `--shared-mem-path` is wired into `qemu_handback.hpp` (currently it isn't) |

Implication: every Tier-1 fixture must encode pass/fail into a **GPR before the exit csrwi**, then in the QEMU 2 phase (after the csrwi, where DPC lands) verify and ship the result through `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`. We cannot use FP/CSR/V state as test channels yet.

### 8.3 Fixture three-phase template

Every new fixture follows this layout (text base `0x80000000`, semihosting param block at `0x80001000` per the existing convention):

```

lastPrompt: read hybrid_plan.md phase 4, add a new option to switch in between. (but also keep the current approach) rewrite the e2e test cases to support two approaches. use max effort

content.content:
300	_start:                          // PHASE A: runs in QEMU 1 (functional)
301	    <set up initial state>
302	    csrwi   0x7C0, 0             // QEMU drains x1..x31+pc, exits
303	
304	phase_b:                         // PHASE B: runs in vsim (RTL, cycle-accurate)
305	    <transform state under test>
306	    csrwi   0x7C0, 1             // vsim drains, exits 200
307	                                 //   on the way out, main spawns QEMU 2
308	                                 //   gdbstub restores x1..x31+pc, sends c
309	phase_c:                         // PHASE C: runs in QEMU 2 (functional)
310	    <fold result into checksum/bitmask in t2>
311	    li      t0, 0x80001000       // semihosting param block
312	    li      t1, 0x20026          // ADP_Stopped_ApplicationExit
313	    sd      t1, 0(t0)
314	    sd      t2, 8(t0)            // exit_code = checksum (0 == pass)
315	    li      a0, 0x20             // SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED
316	    mv      a1, t0
317	    .option push; .option norvc
318	    slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7
319	    .option pop
320	1:  j 1b
321	```
322	
323	The single observable signal remains the QEMU 2 process exit code. Per fixture, the bash driver asserts that code matches an expected value (usually 0).
324	
325	### 8.4 Tier-1 test inventory (no infrastructure changes beyond fixtures)
326	
327	| Fixture | What it verifies | Pass criterion |
328	|---|---|---|
329	| `rt_all_gprs.S` | All 31 GPRs survive QEMU1->vsim->QEMU2. Phase A loads `x_i = 0xA000_0000_0000_0000 + i` for `i=1..31`. Phase B XORs every `x_i` with mask `K=0xDEADBEEFCAFEBABE`. Phase C checks each `x_i == (0xA000_0000_0000_0000+i) ^ K` and sets bit `i` in `t2` on mismatch. | exit 0 (no failed register) |
330	| `rt_abi_aliases.S` | Pass-through fidelity for `ra(x1)`, `sp(x2)`, `gp(x3)`, `tp(x4)`, `fp(x8)`. Phase A sets each to a distinct page-aligned constant in [0x8010_0000..0x8050_0000]. Phase B does NOT touch them (only csrwi entry+exit). Phase C verifies every alias unchanged; exit-code = first-mismatch index or 0. | exit 0 |
331	| `rt_sign_bits.S` | 64-bit width fidelity through DM Abstract Commands. Phase A loads `x10..x14` with `0`, `-1`, `0x8000_0000_0000_0000`, `0x7FFF_FFFF_FFFF_FFFF`, `0x0000_0000_FFFF_FFFF`. Phase B is a single nop. Phase C compares element-wise. | exit 0 (no truncation/sign-ext bug) |
332	| `rt_pc_jump.S` | PC fidelity at the exit boundary. Phase B does `j .+0x40` so the exit csrwi sits 64 bytes ahead of where Phase B started; verifies DPC captured by `state_drain` reflects the post-jump PC, and QEMU 2 lands on Phase C at the right offset. Phase C just exits 0. Wrong PC -> illegal insn / hang -> bash timeout fails the test. | exit 0 |
333	| `rt_runtime_kind.S` | The `HYBRID_HANDOFF_RUNTIME` trigger path (`csrrw zero,0x7C0,x10` where `x10=1`). Phase A: `li x10, 1`, then `csrrw zero, 0x7C0, x10` (exit-via-runtime form). Wait - this fires at QEMU first, not vsim. Better split: Phase A uses `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` to enter; Phase B has `li x10,1; csrrw x0,0x7C0,x10` to exit through the runtime decode in `handoff_controller.hpp:42-44`. Verifies `decode_csrrw_rs1` + GPR-read-by-rs1 path. | exit 0 |
334	| `rt_long_roi.S` | Vsim runs a non-trivial ROI: 100-iteration counted loop summing `1..100` into `x10` (expected 5050). Phase B is the loop; Phase C verifies `x10 == 5050`. Catches resume-state corruption only visible after many cycles, and exercises branch/decode through the cycle-accurate pipeline. | exit 0 |
335	| `rt_mem_loadstore.S` | Vsim memory subsystem under load while resumed from foreign state. Phase A initializes a 64-byte buffer at `0x8000_2000` to a known pattern via `sd` from x10. Phase B reads the buffer back word-by-word, XORs with a constant, writes back, then re-reads and folds into x10 (final checksum). Phase C compares x10 to the pre-computed expected. Exercises load-after-store, RAW within vsim. | exit 0 |
336	| `rt_unaligned_lh.S` | Halfword/byte loads at unaligned addresses inside vsim ROI. Phase A stores 0xCAFEBABEDEADBEEF at 0x8000_3000 and 0xCAFEBABEDEADBEEF at 0x8000_3008. Phase B does `lh x10, 1(t0)`, `lb x11, 3(t0)`, `lhu x12, 5(t0)`, accumulates into x10. Phase C compares against expected. | exit 0 |
337	
338	### 8.5 Tier-2 test (requires Phase 1 wiring into handback)
339	
340	**`rt_shared_mem.sh`**: cross-simulator memory survival via mmap. This is the only test that detects regressions in "memory written in vsim is visible to QEMU 2" - the key promise of the hybrid architecture beyond Phase 3.
341	
342	Required code change (small, but a real change beyond test scaffolding):
343	- `src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp:84-116` (`detail::spawn_qemu`) accepts an optional `shared_mem_path`. When set, `argv` is rebuilt to add `-object memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=<path>,size=<size>` and `-machine virt,memory-backend=mem` (replacing `-M virt`).
344	- `src/main.cpp` plumbs the existing `--shared-mem-path` / `--shared-mem-size` flags into `run_qemu_handback`.
345	- Bash driver creates `/dev/shm/vsim-rt-shared` of the right size, launches QEMU 1 with `-object memory-backend-file,...,share=on`, vsim with `--shared-mem-path`, and asserts.
346	
347	Fixture (`rt_shared_mem.S`):
348	- Phase A: write magic byte pattern (e.g., `0xA5` x 4096) to `0x8001_0000` from x10, csrwi entry.
349	- Phase B: vsim verifies pattern is intact (loop reading 4 KB into checksum), XORs page in place with `0x5A`, stores result of mask check to x10, csrwi exit.
350	- Phase C: QEMU 2 reads the same 4 KB through the shared mmap, recomputes checksum (should match `0xA5 ^ 0x5A` = `0xFF` everywhere), folds mismatch count into exit code.
351	
352	Pass criterion: exit 0 AND wall-clock confirms two-sided visibility (page must be modified by both simulators).
353	
354	> **NOTE**: Tier-2 is currently **blocked** by the memory-layout mismatch described in Section 9. The fixture, driver, Makefile entry, and CMake entry are already in tree; the actual e2e will only PASS after Section 9's redesign lands.

361	| `rt_no_qemu_binary.sh` | Same ELF; pass `--qemu-binary /nonexistent`. Vsim's drain still fires; the spawn fails. Verify exit code is propagated as `127` (exec failure), not a hang. | exit 127, no zombie left |
362	| `rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh` | Provide a stub binary that opens the gdb port but never responds. Vsim's `connect_loopback`+session reads must time out within 5 seconds and exit non-zero, not hang the CTest run. | exit != 0, runtime < 30 s |

461	
462	## 9. Cross-Simulator Shared Memory Redesign (Tier-2 Unblocking)
463	
464	### 9.1 Context (why we are redesigning)
465	
466	Section 8.5 promised a Tier-2 e2e that maps the same backing file across QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2. The structural plumbing landed (`spawn_qemu` accepts `shared_mem_path`/`shared_mem_size`, `main.cpp` forwards both) but the test fails at vsim startup with:
467	
468	```
469	[critical] fatal exception: SimpleMemory external_buffer smaller than region size
470	```
471	
472	Root cause is a layout mismatch between vsim's "RAM is everything from 0" model and QEMU's "RAM starts at 0x80000000" model:
473	
474	| | RAM region | data[0] = GPA | GPA 0x80010000 lands at file offset |
475	|---|---|---|---|
476	| vsim main_memory (today) | [0x0, 0x8FFFFFFF] = 2.25 GB (`memory_map.h:8-9`, `platform.hpp:74`) | 0x0 | 0x80010000 |
477	| QEMU virt machine | [0x80000000, 0x80000000+N) (`qemu_v5/hw/riscv/virt.c` VIRT_DRAM=0x80000000) | 0x80000000 | 0x10000 |
478	
479	Two derived symptoms:
480	- `simple_memory.hpp:60-70` rejects external buffers smaller than the full 2.25 GB region, so any non-trivial mmap size fails before bytes are exchanged.
481	- `simulator.hpp:246` calls `read_elf_into_memory(*elf, xlen_t{0}, ...)` with a hardcoded base of 0; correctness depends on `region.base_address == 0`.
482	
483	The intended outcome of this redesign: vsim and QEMU agree on which file byte backs which GPA, with no extra owned storage, and with the cycle-accurate path unchanged in non-hybrid mode.
484	
485	### 9.2 Design (correctness first, then configurable)
486	
487	**Principle**: in hybrid mode the mmap window IS the addressable RAM. The vsim `main_memory` region is narrowed at construction time to `[base_gpa, base_gpa + size)` where `base_gpa` matches QEMU virt's RAM base (0x80000000). No new "data origin within a wider region" abstraction; the region itself moves.
488	
489	```
490	NON-HYBRID (preserved verbatim - default constructor path)
491	  Platform(ram_buffer = {})
492	    region        = [RAM_BASE = 0x0, RAM_END = 0x8FFFFFFF]   // 2.25 GB
493	    storage       = owned vector, 2.25 GB
494	    ELF load base = region.base_address = 0x0
495	    ELF segment at 0x80000000 -> data[0x80000000]            // works as today
496	
497	HYBRID (--shared-mem-path P --shared-mem-size N [--shared-mem-base B = 0x80000000])
498	  MmapBacking mmap(P, N, base_gpa = B)
499	  Platform(ram_buffer = mmap.span(), ram_region = [B, B + N - 1])
500	    region        = [B, B + N - 1]                            // narrow window
501	    storage       = external mmap span, exactly N bytes       // size check passes
502	    ELF load base = region.base_address = B = 0x80000000
503	    ELF segment at 0x80000000 -> data[0]                      // file offset 0
504	    GPA 0x80010000 -> data[0x10000] = file offset 0x10000     // matches QEMU
505	    Alias region narrowed to {0,0} (degenerate -> contains() always false)
506	```
507	
508	`--shared-mem-base` defaults to 0x80000000 so plain `--shared-mem-path X --shared-mem-size N` Just Works. The flag exists so that future QEMU machine types or alternate test harnesses can override without code changes — this is the "configurable" half of the requirement.
509	
510	**Alternative considered and rejected**: keep the wide [0, 2.25 GB) region and add a `data_origin` field plus owned-fallback for non-mapped GPAs. Rejected because (a) hybrid mode never needs RAM outside the mmap window — anything below 0x80000000 in QEMU virt is MMIO/ROM, not RAM, and accessing it from vsim while purporting to share with QEMU is already undefined; (b) a sub-range data window doubles the SimpleMemory state machine (mapped vs owned per access); (c) it conflicts with the existing "external_buffer must cover the whole region" contract that other call sites rely on.
511	
512	### 9.3 Code changes
513	
514	**Modify**:
515	
516	| File | Change | TDD phase |
517	|---|---|---|
518	| `src/platform/mmap_backing.hpp` | Add `uint64_t base_gpa_` field, constructor param `base_gpa = 0x80000000`, getter `base_gpa()`. Storage and span() unchanged. | structural |
519	| `src/platform/platform.hpp:71-75` | Platform constructor adds optional `MemoryRegion ram_region` (default `{RAM_BASE, RAM_END}`) and optional `MemoryRegion ram_alias_region` (default `{RAM_ALIAS_BASE, RAM_ALIAS_END}`). Used in main_memory construction. | structural |
520	| `src/simulator.hpp` (constructor, ~line 106) | Forward optional `ram_region` / `ram_alias_region` to Platform. | structural |
521	| `src/simulator.hpp:246` | `read_elf_into_memory(*elf, xlen_t{0}, ...)` -> `read_elf_into_memory(*elf, platform.main_memory.region.base_address, ...)`. | behavioral |
522	| `src/main.cpp` | New CLI flag `--shared-mem-base <hex>` (default 0x80000000). When `--shared-mem-path` is set, construct `MmapBacking(path, size, base_gpa)`, derive `ram_region = {base_gpa, base_gpa + size - 1}` and `ram_alias_region = {0, 0}`, pass to Simulator. | behavioral |
523

526	- `src/platform/memory_region.hpp` — `contains()` already exclusive-end aware; degenerate `{0,0}` (stored as [0,1)) contains nothing useful but never matches a real GPA.
527	- `src/utils/elf_loader.hpp` — already takes `base_addr` as a parameter; just gets a different value from the call site.
528	- `src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` — `spawn_qemu` already builds `-object memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=...,size=...` plus `-machine virt,memory-backend=mem`; no further change.
529	- `hybrid/test/rt_shared_mem.S`, `tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh`, `hybrid/test/Makefile`, `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` — already in tree from prior session.

file.content:
_start:                          // PHASE A: runs in QEMU 1 (functional)
    <set up initial state>
    csrwi   0x7C0, 0             // QEMU drains x1..x31+pc, exits

phase_b:                         // PHASE B: runs in vsim (RTL, cycle-accurate)
    <transform state under test>
    csrwi   0x7C0, 1             // vsim drains, exits 200
                                 //   on the way out, main spawns QEMU 2
                                 //   gdbstub restores x1..x31+pc, sends c
phase_c:                         // PHASE C: runs in QEMU 2 (functional)
    <fold result into checksum/bitmask in t2>
    li      t0, 0x80001000       // semihosting param block
    li      t1, 0x20026          // ADP_Stopped_ApplicationExit
    sd      t1, 0(t0)
    sd      t2, 8(t0)            // exit_code = checksum (0 == pass)
    li      a0, 0x20             // SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED
    mv      a1, t0
    .option push; .option norvc
    slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7
    .option pop
1:  j 1b
```

The single observable signal remains the QEMU 2 process exit code. Per fixture, the bash driver asserts that code matches an expected value (usually 0).

### 8.4 Tier-1 test inventory (no infrastructure changes beyond fixtures)

| Fixture | What it verifies | Pass criterion |
|---|---|---|
| `rt_all_gprs.S` | All 31 GPRs survive QEMU1->vsim->QEMU2. Phase A loads `x_i = 0xA000_0000_0000_0000 + i` for `i=1..31`. Phase B XORs every `x_i` with mask `K=0xDEADBEEFCAFEBABE`. Phase C checks each `x_i == (0xA000_0000_0000_0000+i) ^ K` and sets bit `i` in `t2` on mismatch. | exit 0 (no failed register) |
| `rt_abi_aliases.S` | Pass-through fidelity for `ra(x1)`, `sp(x2)`, `gp(x3)`, `tp(x4)`, `fp(x8)`. Phase A sets each to a distinct page-aligned constant in [0x8010_0000..0x8050_0000]. Phase B does NOT touch them (only csrwi entry+exit). Phase C verifies every alias unchanged; exit-code = first-mismatch index or 0. | exit 0 |
| `rt_sign_bits.S` | 64-bit width fidelity through DM Abstract Commands. Phase A loads `x10..x14` with `0`, `-1`, `0x8000_0000_0000_0000`, `0x7FFF_FFFF_FFFF_FFFF`, `0x0000_0000_FFFF_FFFF`. Phase B is a single nop. Phase C compares element-wise. | exit 0 (no truncation/sign-ext bug) |
| `rt_pc_jump.S` | PC fidelity at the exit boundary. Phase B does `j .+0x40` so the exit csrwi sits 64 bytes ahead of where Phase B started; verifies DPC captured by `state_drain` reflects the post-jump PC, and QEMU 2 lands on Phase C at the right offset. Phase C just exits 0. Wrong PC -> illegal insn / hang -> bash timeout fails the test. | exit 0 |
| `rt_runtime_kind.S` | The `HYBRID_HANDOFF_RUNTIME` trigger path (`csrrw zero,0x7C0,x10` where `x10=1`). Phase A: `li x10, 1`, then `csrrw zero, 0x7C0, x10` (exit-via-runtime form). Wait - this fires at QEMU first, not vsim. Better split: Phase A uses `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` to enter; Phase B has `li x10,1; csrrw x0,0x7C0,x10` to exit through the runtime decode in `handoff_controller.hpp:42-44`. Verifies `decode_csrrw_rs1` + GPR-read-by-rs1 path. | exit 0 |
| `rt_long_roi.S` | Vsim runs a non-trivial ROI: 100-iteration counted loop summing `1..100` into `x10` (expected 5050). Phase B is the loop; Phase C verifies `x10 == 5050`. Catches resume-state corruption only visible after many cycles, and exercises branch/decode through the cycle-accurate pipeline. | exit 0 |
| `rt_mem_loadstore.S` | Vsim memory subsystem under load while resumed from foreign state. Phase A initializes a 64-byte buffer at `0x8000_2000` to a known pattern via `sd` from x10. Phase B reads the buffer back word-by-word, XORs with a constant, writes back, then re-reads and folds into x10 (final checksum). Phase C compares x10 to the pre-computed expected. Exercises load-after-store, RAW within vsim. | exit 0 |
| `rt_unaligned_lh.S` | Halfword/byte loads at unaligned addresses inside vsim ROI. Phase A stores 0xCAFEBABEDEADBEEF at 0x8000_3000 and 0xCAFEBABEDEADBEEF at 0x8000_3008. Phase B does `lh x10, 1(t0)`, `lb x11, 3(t0)`, `lhu x12, 5(t0)`, accumulates into x10. Phase C compares against expected. | exit 0 |

### 8.5 Tier-2 test (requires Phase 1 wiring into handback)

**`rt_shared_mem.sh`**: cross-simulator memory survival via mmap. This is the only test that detects regressions in "memory written in vsim is visible to QEMU 2" - the key promise of the hybrid architecture beyond Phase 3.

Required code change (small, but a real change beyond test scaffolding):
- `src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp:84-116` (`detail::spawn_qemu`) accepts an optional `shared_mem_path`. When set, `argv` is rebuilt to add `-object memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=<path>,size=<size>` and `-machine virt,memory-backend=mem` (replacing `-M virt`).
- `src/main.cpp` plumbs the existing `--shared-mem-path` / `--shared-mem-size` flags into `run_qemu_handback`.
- Bash driver creates `/dev/shm/vsim-rt-shared` of the right size, launches QEMU 1 with `-object memory-backend-file,...,share=on`, vsim with `--shared-mem-path`, and asserts.

Fixture (`rt_shared_mem.S`):
- Phase A: write magic byte pattern (e.g., `0xA5` x 4096) to `0x8001_0000` from x10, csrwi entry.
- Phase B: vsim verifies pattern is intact (loop reading 4 KB into checksum), XORs page in place with `0x5A`, stores result of mask check to x10, csrwi exit.
- Phase C: QEMU 2 reads the same 4 KB through the shared mmap, recomputes checksum (should match `0xA5 ^ 0x5A` = `0xFF` everywhere), folds mismatch count into exit code.

Pass criterion: exit 0 AND wall-clock confirms two-sided visibility (page must be modified by both simulators).

> **NOTE**: Tier-2 is currently **blocked** by the memory-layout mismatch described in Section 9. The fixture, driver, Makefile entry, and CMake entry are already in tree; the actual e2e will only PASS after Section 9's redesign lands.

### 8.6 Negative / robustness tests (one-shot, sanity)

| Fixture / driver | What it verifies | Pass criterion |
|---|---|---|
| `rt_corrupt_state.sh` | Reuses a Tier-1 ELF but feeds vsim a truncated `state.bin`. Vsim must fail-fast with a non-200, non-zero exit and a recognizable log line, not segfault or hang. | vsim exit != 200, != 0; stderr matches `state file too small\|invalid magic` |
| `rt_no_qemu_binary.sh` | Same ELF; pass `--qemu-binary /nonexistent`. Vsim's drain still fires; the spawn fails. Verify exit code is propagated as `127` (exec failure), not a hang. | exit 127, no zombie left |
| `rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh` | Provide a stub binary that opens the gdb port but never responds. Vsim's `connect_loopback`+session reads must time out within 5 seconds and exit non-zero, not hang the CTest run. | exit != 0, runtime < 30 s |

These guard against quietly-degrading paths that the happy-path tests can't see.

### 8.7 Infrastructure changes

**`hybrid/test/Makefile`** - add per-fixture build rules. The pattern is mechanical:

```make
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_runtime_kind rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore \
               rt_unaligned_lh rt_shared_mem

**`tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh`** - generalize to take a fixture name and an expected exit code. The script keeps its current layout but reads `FIXTURE` and `EXPECT_RC` from env. The single-fixture invocation today becomes `FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3 roundtrip_e2e.sh`.

**`cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`** - replace the single `add_test(test_roundtrip_e2e ...)` with a `foreach` over a list of `(name, expect_rc)` pairs:

```cmake

  "handoff_roundtrip:3"      # existing
  "rt_all_gprs:0"
  "rt_abi_aliases:0"
  "rt_sign_bits:0"
  "rt_pc_jump:0"
  "rt_runtime_kind:0"
  "rt_long_roi:0"
  "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
  "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
)
foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
  string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
  list(GET parts 0 fname)
  list(GET parts 1 erc)
  add_test(
    NAME test_e2e_${fname}
    COMMAND bash -c "FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  )
  set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )
endforeach()

# Tier-2 (separate driver because it sets up /dev/shm and adds CLI flags)
add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_shared_mem
  COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh)
set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_shared_mem PROPERTIES
  SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77 LABELS "hybrid-e2e")

# Negative/robustness
foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout)
  add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES

endforeach()
```

This gives one CTest entry per fixture, individually pass/fail/skip, all under the `hybrid-e2e` label so `ctest -L hybrid-e2e` runs the suite as one unit.

## 9. Cross-Simulator Shared Memory Redesign (Tier-2 Unblocking)

### 9.1 Context (why we are redesigning)

Section 8.5 promised a Tier-2 e2e that maps the same backing file across QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2. The structural plumbing landed (`spawn_qemu` accepts `shared_mem_path`/`shared_mem_size`, `main.cpp` forwards both) but the test fails at vsim startup with:

```
[critical] fatal exception: SimpleMemory external_buffer smaller than region size
```

Root cause is a layout mismatch between vsim's "RAM is everything from 0" model and QEMU's "RAM starts at 0x80000000" model:

| | RAM region | data[0] = GPA | GPA 0x80010000 lands at file offset |
|---|---|---|---|
| vsim main_memory (today) | [0x0, 0x8FFFFFFF] = 2.25 GB (`memory_map.h:8-9`, `platform.hpp:74`) | 0x0 | 0x80010000 |
| QEMU virt machine | [0x80000000, 0x80000000+N) (`qemu_v5/hw/riscv/virt.c` VIRT_DRAM=0x80000000) | 0x80000000 | 0x10000 |

Two derived symptoms:
- `simple_memory.hpp:60-70` rejects external buffers smaller than the full 2.25 GB region, so any non-trivial mmap size fails before bytes are exchanged.
- `simulator.hpp:246` calls `read_elf_into_memory(*elf, xlen_t{0}, ...)` with a hardcoded base of 0; correctness depends on `region.base_address == 0`.

The intended outcome of this redesign: vsim and QEMU agree on which file byte backs which GPA, with no extra owned storage, and with the cycle-accurate path unchanged in non-hybrid mode.

### 9.2 Design (correctness first, then configurable)

**Principle**: in hybrid mode the mmap window IS the addressable RAM. The vsim `main_memory` region is narrowed at construction time to `[base_gpa, base_gpa + size)` where `base_gpa` matches QEMU virt's RAM base (0x80000000). No new "data origin within a wider region" abstraction; the region itself moves.

```
NON-HYBRID (preserved verbatim - default constructor path)
  Platform(ram_buffer = {})
    region        = [RAM_BASE = 0x0, RAM_END = 0x8FFFFFFF]   // 2.25 GB
    storage       = owned vector, 2.25 GB
    ELF load base = region.base_address = 0x0
    ELF segment at 0x80000000 -> data[0x80000000]            // works as today

HYBRID (--shared-mem-path P --shared-mem-size N [--shared-mem-base B = 0x80000000])
  MmapBacking mmap(P, N, base_gpa = B)
  Platform(ram_buffer = mmap.span(), ram_region = [B, B + N - 1])
    region        = [B, B + N - 1]                            // narrow window
    storage       = external mmap span, exactly N bytes       // size check passes
    ELF load base = region.base_address = B = 0x80000000
    ELF segment at 0x80000000 -> data[0]                      // file offset 0
    GPA 0x80010000 -> data[0x10000] = file offset 0x10000     // matches QEMU
    Alias region narrowed to {0,0} (degenerate -> contains() always false)
```

`--shared-mem-base` defaults to 0x80000000 so plain `--shared-mem-path X --shared-mem-size N` Just Works. The flag exists so that future QEMU machine types or alternate test harnesses can override without code changes — this is the "configurable" half of the requirement.

**Alternative considered and rejected**: keep the wide [0, 2.25 GB) region and add a `data_origin` field plus owned-fallback for non-mapped GPAs. Rejected because (a) hybrid mode never needs RAM outside the mmap window — anything below 0x80000000 in QEMU virt is MMIO/ROM, not RAM, and accessing it from vsim while purporting to share with QEMU is already undefined; (b) a sub-range data window doubles the SimpleMemory state machine (mapped vs owned per access); (c) it conflicts with the existing "external_buffer must cover the whole region" contract that other call sites rely on.

### 9.3 Code changes

**Modify**:

| File | Change | TDD phase |
|---|---|---|
| `src/platform/mmap_backing.hpp` | Add `uint64_t base_gpa_` field, constructor param `base_gpa = 0x80000000`, getter `base_gpa()`. Storage and span() unchanged. | structural |
| `src/platform/platform.hpp:71-75` | Platform constructor adds optional `MemoryRegion ram_region` (default `{RAM_BASE, RAM_END}`) and optional `MemoryRegion ram_alias_region` (default `{RAM_ALIAS_BASE, RAM_ALIAS_END}`). Used in main_memory construction. | structural |
| `src/simulator.hpp` (constructor, ~line 106) | Forward optional `ram_region` / `ram_alias_region` to Platform. | structural |
| `src/simulator.hpp:246` | `read_elf_into_memory(*elf, xlen_t{0}, ...)` -> `read_elf_into_memory(*elf, platform.main_memory.region.base_address, ...)`. | behavioral |
| `src/main.cpp` | New CLI flag `--shared-mem-base <hex>` (default 0x80000000). When `--shared-mem-path` is set, construct `MmapBacking(path, size, base_gpa)`, derive `ram_region = {base_gpa, base_gpa + size - 1}` and `ram_alias_region = {0, 0}`, pass to Simulator. | behavioral |

**Reuse without modification**:
- `src/platform/simple_memory.hpp` — translation `addr - region.base_address` already works for any region base; no change.
- `src/platform/memory_region.hpp` — `contains()` already exclusive-end aware; degenerate `{0,0}` (stored as [0,1)) contains nothing useful but never matches a real GPA.
- `src/utils/elf_loader.hpp` — already takes `base_addr` as a parameter; just gets a different value from the call site.
- `src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` — `spawn_qemu` already builds `-object memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=...,size=...` plus `-machine virt,memory-backend=mem`; no further change.
- `hybrid/test/rt_shared_mem.S`, `tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh`, `hybrid/test/Makefile`, `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` — already in tree from prior session.

**Create**:
- `tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp` — RED unit test for the redesigned path (see 9.4).

### 9.4 TDD sequence

Each step is one commit; commit messages start with `structural:` or `behavioral:` per `~/personal_git/.claude/TDD.md`.

**Step 1 — STRUCTURAL** (`structural: parameterize Platform RAM region for shared-mem mode`):
- Add `base_gpa_` to MmapBacking with default 0x80000000.
- Plumb optional `ram_region` / `ram_alias_region` through Simulator -> Platform.
- All defaults preserve existing behavior. `simulator.hpp:246` still uses hardcoded 0 in this commit.
- Run full `ctest`: every existing test passes (no behavioral change yet).

**Step 2 — RED** (`tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp`):
- Allocate a temp 8 MB file (smaller than current 2.25 GB region; this alone proves the size-check breaks today).
- Construct Platform via `Simulator` with `ram_region = {0x80000000, 0x807FFFFF}`, `ram_buffer = mmap.span()`.
- Issue a SimpleMemory write via the SystemC TLM path at GPA 0x80010000 with a known 8-byte pattern.
- mmap the same file independently (read-only) and assert byte-at-file-offset 0x10000 equals the pattern.
- Test must FAIL on `main` (size-check) and FAIL after Step 1 alone (ELF loader not used in this unit test, but the size-check will already pass after Step 1; the assert that 0x80010000 lands at file offset 0x10000 is what genuinely fails until Step 3).

**Step 3 — GREEN / BEHAVIORAL** (`behavioral: load ELF at region.base_address; honor shared-mem-base in CLI`):
- Change `simulator.hpp:246` to use `platform.main_memory.region.base_address`.
- Add `--shared-mem-base` CLI flag in `main.cpp`, derive `ram_region` from MmapBacking when shared mode is active, narrow alias region to `{0,0}`.
- Step 2's unit test now passes.
- Run `bash tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh`: Phase A fills 4 KB, Phase B verifies + XORs to 0xFF, Phase C confirms via shared mmap, exit 0.

**Step 4 — REGRESSION**:
- `ctest -L hybrid-e2e --output-on-failure`: 8 existing Tier-1 round-trip fixtures still PASS (they don't use `--shared-mem-path`, so they exercise the unchanged non-hybrid path); `test_e2e_rt_shared_mem` now PASSES.
- Full non-hybrid `ctest`: bare-metal tests linked at low addresses (`spin.ld` at 0x80, `handoff.ld` at 0x10000) still load and run because the default RAM region remains [0x0, 0x90000000).
- `tests/hybrid/memory_map.test.cpp`: passes (constants in `memory_map.h` unchanged).

### 9.5 Critical files

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `src/platform/mmap_backing.hpp` | modify (add base_gpa) |
| `src/platform/platform.hpp` | modify (constructor accepts optional ram_region) |
| `src/simulator.hpp` | modify (forward ram_region; use region.base_address as ELF load base) |
| `src/main.cpp` | modify (--shared-mem-base flag; derive ram_region in hybrid mode) |
| `tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp` | create (RED then GREEN unit test) |
| `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` | already wires `test_e2e_rt_shared_mem`; no change |
| `tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh`, `hybrid/test/rt_shared_mem.S`, `hybrid/test/Makefile` | already in tree; no change |

### 9.6 Verification

End-to-end ladder (each step requires the previous):

1. **Unit (RED -> GREEN)**: `tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp` — write at GPA 0x80010000 via SimpleMemory, observe at file offset 0x10000 via independent mmap.
2. **Tier-2 e2e**: `ctest -R test_e2e_rt_shared_mem` — round-trip exits 0; QEMU 1 fills 4 KB with 0xA5..., vsim verifies + XORs to 0xFF..., QEMU 2 confirms.
3. **Tier-1 regression**: `ctest -L hybrid-e2e` — all 8 existing rt_* fixtures still PASS.
4. **Non-hybrid regression**: full `ctest` (no `--shared-mem-path`) — `spin`, `handoff`, `handoff_exit`, kanata, pipeview, all unit tests pass.
5. **Diagnostic**: deliberately set `--shared-mem-base 0x90000000` (wrong); rt_shared_mem.sh fails with a clear "ELF segment at 0x80000000 outside region" error rather than silent corruption.

### 9.7 Out of scope (documented, not blocked-on)

- The 64-bit alias region [0x800000000, 0x880000000): QEMU virt has no alias mirror. In hybrid mode the alias is degenerate; any access to it is undefined behavior, consistent with the existing "MMIO inside ROI" rule in Section 5 / Phase 5.
- Sharing ILM/DLM across simulators: separate regions, separate mmap, separate plan.
- Alternative QEMU machine types with non-0x80000000 RAM bases: enabled by `--shared-mem-base` flag but not exercised by Tier-2.
- mcycle/minstret continuity across the handoff: already documented as a known discontinuity in Section 5.
