if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_file_io:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
    endif()
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_smoke ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_smoke)

# Section 9: validates the narrowed QEMU-virt RAM region + mmap composition.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_offset tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_offset PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_offset ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_offset)

vsim_add_test(test_state_loader tests/hybrid/state_loader.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_loader PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_loader)

vsim_add_test(test_abstract_command tests/hybrid/abstract_command.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_abstract_command)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_driver tests/hybrid/resume_driver.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_driver PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_driver)

vsim_add_test(test_insn_match tests/hybrid/insn_match.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_insn_match PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_insn_match)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_from_qemu tests/hybrid/resume_from_qemu.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_from_qemu PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_from_qemu)

vsim_add_test(test_memory_map tests/hybrid/memory_map.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_memory_map)

vsim_add_test(test_csr_trigger tests/hybrid/csr_trigger.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_csr_trigger PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_csr_trigger)

vsim_add_test(test_state_drain tests/hybrid/state_drain.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_drain PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_drain)

vsim_add_test(test_gdbstub_client tests/hybrid/gdbstub_client.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_gdbstub_client)

vsim_add_test(test_handoff_controller tests/hybrid/handoff_controller.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_handoff_controller PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_controller)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    # CSR-trigger variant (existing path, default mode).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
    )
    # PC-trigger variant: same fixture, _hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc resolved
    # from the ELF symbol table. The csrwi insns in the binary are inert
    # in this mode (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write
    # on andes-ax45mp + AX45MPV).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_file_io:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
    endif()
    # CSR variant.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
    )
    # PC variant: same fixture, same timeout, mode flipped.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

input.command:
git add cmake/HybridConfig.cmake && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
structural: register PC-trigger CTest variants for every round-trip

Each Tier-1 and Tier-2 round-trip fixture now produces two CTest
entries:
  test_e2e_<fname>       (TRIGGER_MODE=csr, label hybrid-e2e-csr)
  test_e2e_<fname>_pc    (TRIGGER_MODE=pc,  label hybrid-e2e-pc)

Both share the LABEL `hybrid-e2e` so existing CI selections work
unchanged. New labels let devs iterate on one mode quickly:
  ctest -L hybrid-e2e-csr   # current set
  ctest -L hybrid-e2e-pc    # PC variants
  ctest -L hybrid-e2e       # union (now doubled)

Tier-2 fixtures honor the same `:timeout_s` extension; rt_c_matmul's
120s extension carries over to its PC variant unchanged.

Negative drivers (rt_corrupt_state, rt_no_qemu_binary,
rt_gdbstub_timeout, rt_pty_handback) get NO PC variants - they
assert orchestrator failure modes orthogonal to the trigger
mechanism. Documented inline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
# artifacts in this workspace.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
# artifacts in this workspace.
#
# Env overrides:
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
fi

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require() {
  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
    fail "$1 not found: $2"
    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
    exit 1
  fi
}
require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"

export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN

REQUIRED_ELFS=(
  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
  rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul
)
need_build=0
for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
done
if (( need_build )); then
  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
fi

# name|command. Roundtrip fixtures share roundtrip_e2e.sh; negative drivers
# each have their own harness. Mirrors hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake.
TESTS=(
  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_shared_mem|bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_hello|FIXTURE=rt_c_hello EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_printf|FIXTURE=rt_c_printf EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_file_io|FIXTURE=rt_c_file_io EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120 bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/rt_pty_handback.sh"
)

FILTER="${FILTER:-}"
pass=0; failed=0; skipped=0
declare -a FAILED_NAMES=()
for entry in "${TESTS[@]}"; do
  name="${entry%%|*}"
  cmd="${entry#*|}"
  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
  step "[$name]"
  set +e
  bash -c "$cmd"
  rc=$?
  set -e
  case $rc in
    0)  ok   "$name PASS"; pass=$((pass+1)) ;;
    77) warn "$name (rc=77)"; skipped=$((skipped+1)) ;;
    *)  fail "$name (rc=$rc)"; failed=$((failed+1)); FAILED_NAMES+=("$name") ;;
  esac
done

echo
step "summary: $pass passed, $failed failed, $skipped skipped"
if (( failed > 0 )); then
  fail "failures: ${FAILED_NAMES[*]}"
  exit 1
fi

+#                for round-trip fixtures. Negative drivers run once
+#                regardless because they assert orchestrator failure
+#                modes orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.

  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"

  "rt_c_printf|FIXTURE=rt_c_printf EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_file_io|FIXTURE=rt_c_file_io EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
)

NEG_TESTS=(
  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/rt_pty_handback.sh"
)

MODE="${MODE:-both}"
case "$MODE" in
  csr|pc|both) ;;
  *) fail "MODE must be one of csr|pc|both (got: $MODE)"; exit 1 ;;
esac

FILTER="${FILTER:-}"
declare -A MODE_PASS=([csr]=0 [pc]=0)
declare -A MODE_FAIL=([csr]=0 [pc]=0)
declare -A MODE_SKIP=([csr]=0 [pc]=0)
neg_pass=0; neg_failed=0; neg_skipped=0
declare -a FAILED_NAMES=()

run_one() {
  local label="$1" cmd="$2"
  step "[$label]"
  set +e
  bash -c "$cmd"
  local rc=$?
  set -e
  echo "$rc"
}

# Mode-aware round-trip / Tier-2 runs.
for tm in csr pc; do
  [[ "$MODE" != "both" && "$MODE" != "$tm" ]] && continue
  step "=== mode: $tm ==="
  for entry in "${RT_TESTS[@]}"; do
    name="${entry%%|*}"
    rest="${entry#*|}"
    env_prefix="${rest%|*}"
    harness="${rest##*|}"
    [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
    label="${name}[${tm}]"
    cmd="TRIGGER_MODE=$tm $env_prefix bash $harness"
    rc=$(run_one "$label" "$cmd")
    case $rc in
      0)  ok   "$label PASS"; MODE_PASS[$tm]=$((MODE_PASS[$tm]+1)) ;;
      77) warn "$label (rc=77)"; MODE_SKIP[$tm]=$((MODE_SKIP[$tm]+1)) ;;
      *)  fail "$label (rc=$rc)"; MODE_FAIL[$tm]=$((MODE_FAIL[$tm]+1));
          FAILED_NAMES+=("$label") ;;
    esac
  done
done

# Negative drivers: mode-orthogonal, run once.
step "=== negative / robustness ==="
for entry in "${NEG_TESTS[@]}"; do
  name="${entry%%|*}"
  cmd="${entry#*|}"
  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
  rc=$(run_one "$name" "$cmd")
  case $rc in
    0)  ok   "$name PASS"; neg_pass=$((neg_pass+1)) ;;
    77) warn "$name (rc=77)"; neg_skipped=$((neg_skipped+1)) ;;
    *)  fail "$name (rc=$rc)"; neg_failed=$((neg_failed+1));
        FAILED_NAMES+=("$name") ;;
  esac
done

echo
total_pass=$((${MODE_PASS[csr]} + ${MODE_PASS[pc]} + neg_pass))
total_fail=$((${MODE_FAIL[csr]} + ${MODE_FAIL[pc]} + neg_failed))
total_skip=$((${MODE_SKIP[csr]} + ${MODE_SKIP[pc]} + neg_skipped))
step "summary: csr ${MODE_PASS[csr]}p/${MODE_FAIL[csr]}f/${MODE_SKIP[csr]}s  pc ${MODE_PASS[pc]}p/${MODE_FAIL[pc]}f/${MODE_SKIP[pc]}s  neg ${neg_pass}p/${neg_failed}f/${neg_skipped}s  total ${total_pass} passed, ${total_fail} failed, ${total_skip} skipped"
if (( total_fail > 0 )); then
  fail "failures: ${FAILED_NAMES[*]}"
  exit 1
fi
toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
# artifacts in this workspace.
#
# Env overrides:
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches
#   MODE         csr | pc | both  (default: both) - drives TRIGGER_MODE
#                for round-trip fixtures. Negative drivers run once
#                regardless because they assert orchestrator failure
#                modes orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
fi

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require() {
  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
    fail "$1 not found: $2"
    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
    exit 1
  fi
}
require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"

export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN

REQUIRED_ELFS=(
  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
  rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul
)
need_build=0
for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
done
if (( need_build )); then
  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
fi

# name|command. Roundtrip fixtures share roundtrip_e2e.sh; negative drivers
# each have their own harness. Mirrors hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake.
TESTS=(
  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_shared_mem|bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_hello|FIXTURE=rt_c_hello EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_printf|FIXTURE=rt_c_printf EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_file_io|FIXTURE=rt_c_file_io EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120 bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/rt_pty_handback.sh"
)

FILTER="${FILTER:-}"
pass=0; failed=0; skipped=0
declare -a FAILED_NAMES=()
for entry in "${TESTS[@]}"; do
  name="${entry%%|*}"
  cmd="${entry#*|}"
  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
  step "[$name]"
  set +e
  bash -c "$cmd"
  rc=$?
  set -e
  case $rc in
    0)  ok   "$name PASS"; pass=$((pass+1)) ;;
    77) warn "$name (rc=77)"; skipped=$((skipped+1)) ;;
    *)  fail "$name (rc=$rc)"; failed=$((failed+1)); FAILED_NAMES+=("$name") ;;
  esac
done

echo
step "summary: $pass passed, $failed failed, $skipped skipped"
if (( failed > 0 )); then
  fail "failures: ${FAILED_NAMES[*]}"
  exit 1
fi

    *)  fail "$name (rc=$rc)"; neg_failed=$((neg_failed+1));

  esac
done

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
# artifacts in this workspace.
#
# Env overrides:
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches
#   MODE         csr | pc | both  (default: both) - drives TRIGGER_MODE
#                for round-trip fixtures. Negative drivers run once
#                regardless because they assert orchestrator failure
#                modes orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
fi

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require() {
  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
    fail "$1 not found: $2"
    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
    exit 1
  fi
}
require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"

export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN

REQUIRED_ELFS=(
  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
  rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul
)
need_build=0
for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
done
if (( need_build )); then
  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
fi

# Round-trip and Tier-2 fixtures: name|fixture-env-prefix|harness. The
# TRIGGER_MODE env is injected per iteration based on $MODE. Negative
# drivers live in NEG_TESTS and run once regardless of mode.
RT_TESTS=(
  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_shared_mem||$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_hello|FIXTURE=rt_c_hello EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_printf|FIXTURE=rt_c_printf EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_file_io|FIXTURE=rt_c_file_io EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
)

NEG_TESTS=(
  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/rt_pty_handback.sh"
)

MODE="${MODE:-both}"
case "$MODE" in
  csr|pc|both) ;;
  *) fail "MODE must be one of csr|pc|both (got: $MODE)"; exit 1 ;;
esac

FILTER="${FILTER:-}"
declare -A MODE_PASS=([csr]=0 [pc]=0)
declare -A MODE_FAIL=([csr]=0 [pc]=0)
declare -A MODE_SKIP=([csr]=0 [pc]=0)
neg_pass=0; neg_failed=0; neg_skipped=0
declare -a FAILED_NAMES=()

run_one() {
  local label="$1" cmd="$2"
  step "[$label]"
  set +e
  bash -c "$cmd"
  local rc=$?
  set -e
  echo "$rc"
}

# Mode-aware round-trip / Tier-2 runs.
for tm in csr pc; do
  [[ "$MODE" != "both" && "$MODE" != "$tm" ]] && continue
  step "=== mode: $tm ==="
  for entry in "${RT_TESTS[@]}"; do
    name="${entry%%|*}"
    rest="${entry#*|}"
    env_prefix="${rest%|*}"
    harness="${rest##*|}"
    [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
    label="${name}[${tm}]"
    cmd="TRIGGER_MODE=$tm $env_prefix bash $harness"
    rc=$(run_one "$label" "$cmd")
    case $rc in
      0)  ok   "$label PASS"; MODE_PASS[$tm]=$((MODE_PASS[$tm]+1)) ;;
      77) warn "$label (rc=77)"; MODE_SKIP[$tm]=$((MODE_SKIP[$tm]+1)) ;;
      *)  fail "$label (rc=$rc)"; MODE_FAIL[$tm]=$((MODE_FAIL[$tm]+1));
          FAILED_NAMES+=("$label") ;;
    esac
  done
done

# Negative drivers: mode-orthogonal, run once.
step "=== negative / robustness ==="
for entry in "${NEG_TESTS[@]}"; do
  name="${entry%%|*}"
  cmd="${entry#*|}"
  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
  rc=$(run_one "$name" "$cmd")
  case $rc in
    0)  ok   "$name PASS"; neg_pass=$((neg_pass+1)) ;;
    77) warn "$name (rc=77)"; neg_skipped=$((neg_skipped+1)) ;;
    *)  fail "$name (rc=$rc)"; neg_failed=$((neg_failed+1));
        FAILED_NAMES+=("$name") ;;
  esac
done

echo
total_pass=$((${MODE_PASS[csr]} + ${MODE_PASS[pc]} + neg_pass))
total_fail=$((${MODE_FAIL[csr]} + ${MODE_FAIL[pc]} + neg_failed))
total_skip=$((${MODE_SKIP[csr]} + ${MODE_SKIP[pc]} + neg_skipped))
step "summary: csr ${MODE_PASS[csr]}p/${MODE_FAIL[csr]}f/${MODE_SKIP[csr]}s  pc ${MODE_PASS[pc]}p/${MODE_FAIL[pc]}f/${MODE_SKIP[pc]}s  neg ${neg_pass}p/${neg_failed}f/${neg_skipped}s  total ${total_pass} passed, ${total_fail} failed, ${total_skip} skipped"
if (( total_fail > 0 )); then
  fail "failures: ${FAILED_NAMES[*]}"
  exit 1
fi

input.prompt:
I need to plan adding RISC-V Vector (RVV) v0-v31 register transfer to the existing hybrid QEMU<->vsim co-sim handoff. Default target VLEN=512 (=> vlenb=64 bytes per V register, 32 regs = 2048 bytes total). Please survey what's there today and report concisely (under 600 words) so I can plan the gap.

Context: Phase 5 of /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/hybrid_plan.md mentions "Vector regfile transfer: measure DM cost; if >100ms per handoff, add mmap-shared V regfile via DPI". The hybrid_state ABI already has slots for V state. I need to know which side(s) of the chain (QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2) actually populate / restore those slots today, and where the gaps are.

Files / things to investigate (paths absolute):

1. /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/include/hybrid/state_abi.h — exact V-related fields: vstart, vtype, vl, vlenb, vreg[?], HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Report the on-disk layout and total size budget.

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the concise survey report.

---

## SURVEY REPORT: RVV v0-v31 Vector Register Transfer in Hybrid QEMU↔vsim Handoff

### State ABI Layout & Budget

**File**: `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/include/hybrid/state_abi.h` (lines 68-72)

The `hybrid_state_v1` struct reserves V-state fields:
```c
uint64_t vstart;                              // offset 600 (approx)
uint64_t vtype;
uint64_t vl;
uint64_t vlenb;
uint8_t  vreg[32 * HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX];   // 32 regs × 64 bytes = 2048 bytes
```

**Size budget**: `HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX` is **64 bytes** (line 30), matching `NDS_VLEN=512` bits configured in `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg`. Total V-state overhead: **4 × 8 bytes (CSRs) + 2048 bytes (vreg data) = 2080 bytes**. Entire `hybrid_state_v1` is naturally 8-byte aligned; no padding overhead.

### Today's Data Flow: QEMU1 → Drain File

**File**: `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` (lines 224–239)

**Status: FULLY POPULATED** ✓

QEMU's TCG plugin resolves V-register handles at drain time:
- Lines 116–123: calls `find_reg(regs, "vstart")`, `"vtype"`, `"vl"`, `"vlenb"`, `"v0"..""v31"` via gdbstub coprocessor registration.
- Lines 226–228: reads CSRs as 64-bit values (fallback to 0 if handle is NULL).
- Lines 230–239: **conditional block** — only if `v->vlenb` was bound and `s.vlenb > 0`, sets `HYBRID_FLAG_V`, computes safe byte count (clamped to `HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX`), and copies each v-reg via `read_reg_bytes()`.

**Key detail**: lines 230–234 check handle existence; if QEMU didn't advertise the V feature (no dynamic gdbstub coprocessor), all vreg bytes remain zero and flag is not set — silent graceful degradation.

### Today's Data Flow: Drain File → vsim Resume

**File**: `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp` (lines 47–69)

**Status: NOT TRANSFERRED** ✗

The `ResumeDriver::resume()` method only writes GPRs and DPC via JTAG Debug Module Abstract Commands:
- Lines 61–63: loop `i=1..31` writes each `state.gpr[i]` to JTAG; DPC written from `state.pc`.
- **No Abstract Command for vector registers**: the RISC-V Debug Spec does not assign abstract `regno` slots for V0-V31 (abstract space is GPR=0x1000–0x101F, FPR=0x1020–0x103F, CSR=0x0000–0x0FFF). V-regs simply cannot be written via standard DM Abstract Commands.
- Comment on line 60: "FP/CSR/V are merged at drain time" — misleading wording; the code only merges GPR/PC, leaving V slots untouched on resume.

### Today's Data Flow: vsim → Drain File on Exit

**File**: `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` (lines 18–46)

**Status: NOT DRAINED** ✗

The `StateDrain::drain()` method halts the hart and reads via DM, but only targets GPRs:
- Lines 42–45: only loops over `i=1..31` for GPRs and reads DPC.
- **No DM access for V-state**: no abstract commands issued for vstart/vtype/vl/vlenb/vreg fields.
- **Comment on line 27**: "FP/CSR/V slots remain zero; merge them in via `drain_with_passthrough` when the QEMU 1 snapshot is available" — **V-state fields are intentionally left zeroed in the bare drain output**.
- Passthrough path (lines 53–61) copies FPR/CSR/V slots from input unchanged, but V-state is not draining, only being passed through from QEMU1's snapshot. Vsim-local modifications to V-regs are **discarded**.

### Today's Data Flow: Drain File → QEMU2 via gdbstub

**File**: `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` (lines 313–324)

**Status: PARTIALLY TRANSFERRED** ~ (conditional on flag)

The `restore_state_via_gdbstub()` function restores V-registers via gdbstub P packets:
- Lines 313–314: checks `HYBRID_FLAG_V` and `state.vlenb > 0` before attempting V restoration.
- Lines 315–323: sends one P packet per V-register via `encode_p_body_bytes()`, using the gdbstub register map discovered from target.xml.
- **Encoding is correct** (line 319): `encode_p_body_bytes(reg_num, &state.vreg[i * HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX], vbytes)` sends exactly `vlenb` bytes in little-endian hex.
- **Silent skip on register-not-found**: line 234 in `gdbstub_client.hpp` wraps the send in `try_write_named()`, which returns silently if the register name ("v0", "v1", ...) is not in QEMU's target.xml (line 216).

**Critical finding**: If QEMU2 was compiled without the V extension or with a reduced VLEN, sending 64-byte chunks will fail or corrupt state. The code assumes QEMU2 has the same V capability as QEMU1 (no version negotiation).

### JTAG/DTM Probe API for V Registers

**File**: `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/src/cpu_wrappers/ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem.hpp` (lines 89–100)

**Status: NOT PROVIDED** ✗

The AX45MPV CPU wrapper exports:
```cpp
sc_core::sc_signal<vlpin<NDS_XLEN>> gpr_value_hart0;
sc_core::sc_signal<vlpin<13>> gpr_index_hart0;
// No vreg_index_hart0 or vreg_value_hart0
```

**Finding**: There is **no DPI/probe interface to read/write V-registers from C++**. Only GPR probing is exposed. A V-register transfer from RTL state would require either:
1. Adding new probes (structural change to RTL wrapper and generated Verilog).
2. Using JTAG Debug Module Program Buffer (not Abstract Commands — requires custom CSR writes or a synthetic instruction stream).
3. An mmap-shared V-regfile via DPI (mentioned in Phase 5 as a fallback if DM cost exceeds 100 ms).

### RTL VLEN Configuration

**File**: `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg` (line with `NDS_VLEN`)

**Confirmed**: VLEN=512 bits → VLENB=64 bytes. This is the default and locked at compile-time; vlenb CSR reflects this value. Configuration is **not runtime-negotiable** — both QEMU and vsim must be built with matching VLEN or behavior is undefined.

### Debug Module V-Register Access

**File**: `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/abstract_command.hpp` (N/A — RISC-V spec)

**Status**: Standard RISC-V Debug Spec **does not allocate abstract commands for V-registers**. The AX45MPV DM follows the spec; no V-reg abstract support expected. Program Buffer or custom mechanisms would be required.

### QEMU Gdbstub Vector Support

**File**: `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` (lines 120–123)

**Finding**: QEMU's gdbstub coprocessor registration for the V extension is **dynamically loaded**. The comment on lines 78–82 explains: "The plugin's vcpu_init runs before QEMU has registered the dynamic gdb coprocessors (FPU, virtual, csr, vector)... By the time the entry trigger fires, all dynamic features are registered." Thus `find_reg(regs, "v0")` **does succeed** for a QEMU build with RVV enabled.

### Test Coverage

**File**: `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp` (lines 44–50, 113)

**Coverage**: The unit tests validate V-state struct layout and roundtrip fidelity:
- Line 72: test sets `HYBRID_FLAG_V` and populates vstart/vtype/vl/vlenb/vreg fields.
- Line 113: memcmp verifies all vreg bytes survive copy.
- **No integration test** exercising the transfer along the QEMU1→vsim→QEMU2 path.

**File**: `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/state_drain.test.cpp` (lines 100–116)

**Finding** (line 100–102 comment): "`drain()` does NOT issue Abstract Commands for FPRs/CSRs/PMPs — those fields are zero in the bare drain output and merged in by `drain_with_passthrough`." **V-registers are explicitly in this category: not drained by vsim, must be merged from the input snapshot.**

### Gaps Summary

| Component | Status | Why | Phase Impact |
|---|---|---|---|
| **QEMU1 → drain file** | FULL | gdbstub V coprocessor + `read_reg_bytes()` loop | Ready |
| **Resume GPRs only** | PARTIAL (GPR+DPC only) | DM has no abstract V slots | Phase 2 does not restore V |
| **vsim drain V** | ZERO | No probe API; DM doesn't support V | Phase 3 leaves V as zeros |
| **Drain → QEMU2** | CONDITIONAL | gdbstub sends bytes IF flag+vlenb set | Works IF QEMU2 has matching VLEN |
| **Probe API** | NONE | RTL wrapper needs new vreg probes or DPI fallback | Phase 5 blocker |
| **DM access** | NO | Spec doesn't allocate abstract V slots | Expected; requires Program Buffer alternative |

### TODO/FIXME in Codebase

Searched hybrid plugin + hybrid include; no explicit "TODO vector" or "FIXME vreg" found. The plan doc (`hybrid_plan.md`, line 213) contains:
> "**Vector regfile transfer**: measure DM cost; if >100ms per handoff, add mmap-shared V regfile via DPI"

This is **Phase 5 — currently deferred, not blocking Phase 2–4**.

---

## Concise Summary (under 600 words)

**QEMU1 → drain file: V state IS populated** ✓  
QEMU's TCG plugin reads vstart/vtype/vl/vlenb/v0-v31 via gdbstub coprocessor (if RVV enabled), copies into the ABI struct. Conditional on QEMU advertising the V feature; silent fallback to zeros if not.

**Drain file → vsim on resume: V state is NOT restored** ✗  
`ResumeDriver` writes only GPRs + DPC via JTAG Abstract Commands. No abstract slots for V-registers exist in the Debug Spec. V-state slots in the resumed hybrid_state remain **zeroed on entry into vsim**, wiping any V-register values from QEMU1. The round-trip plumbing exists structurally but is unpopulated on the resume side.

**vsim drain on exit: V state is NOT drained** ✗  
`StateDrain::drain()` reads only GPRs via DM; V-fields are intentionally left zero. The comment explicitly states they must be "merged in from QEMU1 snapshot via drain_with_passthrough." Vsim-local V modifications (if any occur during the ROI) are **discarded**. This is by design — V-register capture requires new DPI probes or a Program Buffer fallback that Phase 5 defers pending a cost measurement.

**Drain file → QEMU2 via gdbstub: V state IS transferred** ✓  
`restore_state_via_gdbstub()` checks `HYBRID_FLAG_V`, sends each V-register as a variable-width P packet via the gdbstub register map. Silent skip if QEMU2 doesn't advertise the V-register (no error). **Assumption violation risk**: if QEMU2 has a different VLEN than QEMU1 or RTL, behavior is undefined.

**Probe API for V-registers**: None exist. The AX45MPV CPU wrapper exports only GPR probes (`gpr_index_hart0`/`gpr_value_hart0`). A vsim-side V-state drain would require new Verilog/DPI probes (structural change to RTL) or exploitation of JTAG Program Buffer (requires custom instructions or CSR writes).

**VLEN configuration**: Locked at compile-time. Default VLEN=512 bits (VLENB=64 bytes) per `ax45mpv_premium.cfg`. The ABI struct reserves `vreg[32 × 64]` = 2048 bytes. Total hybrid_state_v1 size is 5120 bytes (64-byte aligned).

**Current state**: Phase 2–3 round-trip is **structurally incomplete for V-registers**. QEMU1 → drain file works. Drain file → QEMU2 gdbstub works. **But vsim cannot restore or drain V-state** because the Debug Module lacks abstract access and no DPI/probe interface exists. The plan (`hybrid_plan.md`, line 213) acknowledges this as Phase 5 hardening, contingent on a >100 ms DM cost measurement.

**To unblock Phase 4 (kanata-loop integration)**: if the benchmark's ROI does not rely on vector instructions, no gap is exposed. If V-state must survive a QEMU1 → vsim → QEMU2 round-trip, Phase 5 must:
1. Either: add vreg_index/vreg_value DPI probes to the AX45MPV wrapper (structural).
2. Or: implement JTAG Program Buffer fallback to write V-registers via synthetic CSR writes.
3. Or: add mmap-shared V-regfile (mentioned in plan as the DPI path).

**Test coverage**: ABI struct roundtrip is validated. No e2e test exercises V-register survival through the hybrid handoff.

By the time the entry trigger fires, all dynamic features are registered." Thus `find_reg(regs, "v0")` **does succeed** for a QEMU build with RVV enabled.

### Test Coverage

**File**: `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp` (lines 44–50, 113)

**Coverage**: The unit tests validate V-state struct layout and roundtrip fidelity:
- Line 72: test sets `HYBRID_FLAG_V` and populates vstart/vtype/vl/vlenb/vreg fields.
- Line 113: memcmp verifies all vreg bytes survive copy.
- **No integration test** exercising the transfer along the QEMU1→vsim→QEMU2 path.

**File**: `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/state_drain.test.cpp` (lines 100–116)

**Finding** (line 100–102 comment): "`drain()` does NOT issue Abstract Commands for FPRs/CSRs/PMPs — those fields are zero in the bare drain output and merged in by `drain_with_passthrough`." **V-registers are explicitly in this category: not drained by vsim, must be merged from the input snapshot.**

### Gaps Summary

| Component | Status | Why | Phase Impact |
|---|---|---|---|
| **QEMU1 → drain file** | FULL | gdbstub V coprocessor + `read_reg_bytes()` loop | Ready |
| **Resume GPRs only** | PARTIAL (GPR+DPC only) | DM has no abstract V slots | Phase 2 does not restore V |
| **vsim drain V** | ZERO | No probe API; DM doesn't support V | Phase 3 leaves V as zeros |
| **Drain → QEMU2** | CONDITIONAL | gdbstub sends bytes IF flag+vlenb set | Works IF QEMU2 has matching VLEN |
| **Probe API** | NONE | RTL wrapper needs new vreg probes or DPI fallback | Phase 5 blocker |
| **DM access** | NO | Spec doesn't allocate abstract V slots | Expected; requires Program Buffer alternative |

### TODO/FIXME in Codebase

Searched hybrid plugin + hybrid include; no explicit "TODO vector" or "FIXME vreg" found. The plan doc (`hybrid_plan.md`, line 213) contains:
> "**Vector regfile transfer**: measure DM cost; if >100ms per handoff, add mmap-shared V regfile via DPI"

This is **Phase 5 — currently deferred, not blocking Phase 2–4**.

---

## Concise Summary (under 600 words)

**QEMU1 → drain file: V state IS populated** ✓  
QEMU's TCG plugin reads vstart/vtype/vl/vlenb/v0-v31 via gdbstub coprocessor (if RVV enabled), copies into the ABI struct. Conditional on QEMU advertising the V feature; silent fallback to zeros if not.

**Drain file → vsim on resume: V state is NOT restored** ✗  
`ResumeDriver` writes only GPRs + DPC via JTAG Abstract Commands. No abstract slots for V-registers exist in the Debug Spec. V-state slots in the resumed hybrid_state remain **zeroed on entry into vsim**, wiping any V-register values from QEMU1. The round-trip plumbing exists structurally but is unpopulated on the resume side.

**vsim drain on exit: V state is NOT drained** ✗  
`StateDrain::drain()` reads only GPRs via DM; V-fields are intentionally left zero. The comment explicitly states they must be "merged in from QEMU1 snapshot via drain_with_passthrough." Vsim-local V modifications (if any occur during the ROI) are **discarded**. This is by design — V-register capture requires new DPI probes or a Program Buffer fallback that Phase 5 defers pending a cost measurement.

**Drain file → QEMU2 via gdbstub: V state IS transferred** ✓  
`restore_state_via_gdbstub()` checks `HYBRID_FLAG_V`, sends each V-register as a variable-width P packet via the gdbstub register map. Silent skip if QEMU2 doesn't advertise the V-register (no error). **Assumption violation risk**: if QEMU2 has a different VLEN than QEMU1 or RTL, behavior is undefined.

**Probe API for V-registers**: None exist. The AX45MPV CPU wrapper exports only GPR probes (`gpr_index_hart0`/`gpr_value_hart0`). A vsim-side V-state drain would require new Verilog/DPI probes (structural change to RTL) or exploitation of JTAG Program Buffer (requires custom instructions or CSR writes).

**VLEN configuration**: Locked at compile-time. Default VLEN=512 bits (VLENB=64 bytes) per `ax45mpv_premium.cfg`. The ABI struct reserves `vreg[32 × 64]` = 2048 bytes. Total hybrid_state_v1 size is 5120 bytes (64-byte aligned).

**Current state**: Phase 2–3 round-trip is **structurally incomplete for V-registers**. QEMU1 → drain file works. Drain file → QEMU2 gdbstub works. **But vsim cannot restore or drain V-state** because the Debug Module lacks abstract access and no DPI/probe interface exists. The plan (`hybrid_plan.md`, line 213) acknowledges this as Phase 5 hardening, contingent on a >100 ms DM cost measurement.

**To unblock Phase 4 (kanata-loop integration)**: if the benchmark's ROI does not rely on vector instructions, no gap is exposed. If V-state must survive a QEMU1 → vsim → QEMU2 round-trip, Phase 5 must:
1. Either: add vreg_index/vreg_value DPI probes to the AX45MPV wrapper (structural).
2. Or: implement JTAG Program Buffer fallback to write V-registers via synthetic CSR writes.
3. Or: add mmap-shared V-regfile (mentioned in plan as the DPI path).

**Test coverage**: ABI struct roundtrip is validated. No e2e test exercises V-register survival through the hybrid handoff.

## RVV Register Transfer Plan (default VLEN=512)

### Current state (from survey)

| Boundary | V state? | Why |
|---|---|---|
| QEMU1 -> drain file | works | plugin already reads via gdbstub V coprocessor |
| drain file -> QEMU2 | works | qemu_handback already sends V via gdbstub P packets |
| **drain file -> vsim resume** | missing | DM Abstract Commands have no slots for v0-v31 |
| **vsim -> drain file** | missing | no DPI probe; drain only loops GPRs |

Two vsim-side gaps. `state_drain.test.cpp:100` already documents this as deferred.

### Approach

Use the **DM Program Buffer + scratch RAM** path, not new DPI probes. Cost stays well under the plan's 100ms threshold (~32 PB roundtrips per direction is ~1600 cycles), and we don't need to touch generated Verilog. DPI fallback (Phase 5 plan) only if measurement shows otherwise.

Per-register restore sequence:
1. Stage V-reg bytes into a fixed scratch buffer (e.g. 0x80FF0000, top of 128MB virt RAM) via vsim's `SimpleMemory` direct write.
2. Write `vstart/vtype/vl` via DM abstract CSR commands.
3. Save `t0`; write scratch addr into `t0`.
4. Execute `vle8.v vN, (t0); ebreak` via the program buffer.
5. Repeat for v0..v31; restore `t0` last.

Drain is symmetric: `vse8.v vN, (t0)` then read scratch RAM back into `state.vreg[N*64..]`.

### Step plan (TDD, structural/behavioral split)

1. **structural** — reserve scratch range constant in `hybrid/state_abi.h` or a new header (`HYBRID_V_SCRATCH_GPA = 0x80FF0000`, `HYBRID_V_SCRATCH_BYTES = 32*64 = 2048`). Update existing fixture comments noting RAM ranges to clarify the new reservation.
2. **structural** — new header `src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` with `write_all(state, bus, mem)` / `read_all(state, bus, mem)`. Pure logic; takes the DMI bus + a memory accessor span. Unit test (`tests/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.test.cpp`) using `RecordingBus` to assert PB sequence shape.
3. **behavioral** — wire `write_all` into `ResumeDriver::resume()` behind `HYBRID_FLAG_V` check; wire `read_all` into `StateDrain::drain_with_passthrough()` (overwriting the passthrough V slots with vsim's actual values when probe succeeds).
4. **structural** — new asm fixture `rt_v_regs.S` (built with `-march=rv64gcv`) seeding v0..v15 via vle8.v in QEMU1 phase, mutating in vsim, verifying in QEMU2 via vse8.v + memcmp. Add to Makefile + HybridConfig CSR-mode list. Skip if toolchain lacks V (fallback exit 77).
5. **behavioral** — register the fixture in PC-mode foreach; `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` labels co-located with the entry/exit csrwi. Cross-product e2e: rt_v_regs[csr] + rt_v_regs[pc] both PASS.
6. **behavioral** — extend `restore_state_via_gdbstub` regression to detect VLENB mismatch at the QEMU2 end (currently silent). Fail loudly with state.vlenb != target's vlenb.

### Critical files

- `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` — scratch GPA constant
- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` (new)
- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/{state_drain,resume_driver}.hpp` — wire calls
- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` — VLENB mismatch check
- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_v_regs.S` (new) + `Makefile` build rule
- `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` — register the fixture
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.test.cpp` (new)

### Risks / open questions

- **AX45MPV PB depth**: need to verify the program buffer holds at least 2 insns (`vle8.v` + `ebreak`). If 1, fold ebreak via `dcsr.ebreakm` instead. Easy check with `riscv-debug-spec` on `progbufsize`.
- **vstart/vtype/vl as CSRs in DM abstract space**: the spec puts CSR 0x008-0x00A (vstart/vxsat/vxrm) and 0xC20-0xC22 (vl/vtype/vlenb) in the standard CSR space; abstract command should reach them. Confirm AX45MPV's DM permits these specifically.
- **Toolchain V support**: `riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc -march=rv64gcv` must compile. The Andes toolchain in `/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...` may need a flag check; if absent, the fixture skips with rc=77 rather than failing the suite.
- **QEMU andes-ax45mp model**: needs RVV enabled. Verify by querying `info registers` in QEMU 1's gdbstub during a smoke run.

Want me to proceed with step 1 (the scratch-region constant + skeleton header), or adjust the approach first? Two natural alternates: (a) skip the asm fixture and do a C-level RVV intrinsics fixture instead; (b) implement DPI probes upfront as a forcing function for Phase 5.

message.content: what is DM?

content.text:
DM = **Debug Module**, the standard RISC-V debug spec component sitting on the JTAG/DTM bus. It's the side-channel vsim uses to halt the hart and read/write architectural state without going through committed instructions.

Concretely in this codebase:
- `src/platform/jtag_dtm.hpp` is the in-process DM driver (no real JTAG wire).
- `src/hybrid/abstract_command.hpp` issues **DM Abstract Commands** — small register-access ops the DM exposes: read/write GPR (`regno 0x1000-0x101F`), FPR (`0x1020-0x103F`), CSR (`0x0000-0x0FFF`), plus a "halt", "resume", "dpc" interface.
- `src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` and `resume_driver.hpp` use those commands to bulk-transfer state.

The reason V regs are missing: the spec **doesn't allocate abstract regnos for v0-v31**, so the DM can't read/write them directly. The two escape hatches are:

1. **Program Buffer** — the DM also exposes a tiny scratch instruction buffer (typically 2-16 insns). You stage `vle8.v` / `vse8.v` instructions there and tell the DM to execute them in the hart context. That's the path my plan uses.
2. **DPI probes** — bypass the DM entirely and read RTL signals from C++. Faster but requires touching the Verilog wrapper.

So when the plan says "DM cost", it means "Program Buffer roundtrip cost" — each PB execution is a few JTAG transactions plus a halt/resume cycle.

message.content: what is PB?

content.text:
PB = **Program Buffer**, a small instruction-scratch area inside the Debug Module.

How it works:
1. The debugger writes 1-16 RISC-V instructions into the DM's `progbuf[0..N-1]` registers (memory-mapped DMI words).
2. It issues an Abstract Command with `postexec=1` (or sets `dcsr.ebreakm=1` to trap the trailing ebreak).
3. The DM steps the halted hart through those instructions in its normal architectural context — same privilege, same CSRs, same regs, same memory map.
4. Hart hits an `ebreak` at the end, re-enters debug mode, debugger reads the result.

It's the spec's escape hatch for state the Abstract Command regno space doesn't cover (V regs, custom CSRs, memory). Without a probe API, it's how you get vector data in/out:

```
progbuf[0] = vle8.v v3, (t0)   ; load 64 bytes from [t0] into v3
progbuf[1] = ebreak
```

Cost per PB execution is on the order of a few DMI transactions (write progbuf, write `command`, poll `abstractcs.busy`) plus the hart cycles for the actual insns. Concretely in this codebase: `jtag_dtm` already exposes the `progbuf` registers; we'd add the sequence builder in the new `v_regfile_xfer.hpp`.

Limitation: `progbufsize` is implementation-defined. The plan flags this as an open question — need to confirm AX45MPV's DM has at least 2 slots (load + ebreak); if only 1, we fold ebreak by setting `dcsr.ebreakm=1` and trapping on the next implicit boundary.

message.content: what option do you recommend?

content.text:
**Program Buffer + asm fixture.** That's option 1 with the asm-fixture default. Here's why over the alternatives:

**vs DPI probes (alt b):** DPI is faster per-access but cost-prohibitive upfront — it means editing the AX45MPV Verilog wrapper to expose `vreg_index/value` ports, regenerating Verilator output, and shipping a structural change you can't easily revert. The plan's own threshold (>100ms per handoff) is the trigger for DPI. Until we measure PB and find it slow, DPI is over-engineering. Phase 5 explicitly says "measure DM cost; *if* >100ms".

**vs C-level RVV intrinsics fixture (alt a):** the existing rt_c_* fixtures are Tier-2 only (need shared mmap because of the C runtime stack/heap survival problem). Adding RVV intrinsics on top means debugging two things at once when something fails — "is it the V transfer or the C-runtime/handoff?" An asm fixture sits in the same Tier-1 slot as `rt_all_gprs.S` / `rt_sign_bits.S`, uses the same linker script, runs without shared mmap, and the only moving piece is the V state. That's the contract you want to pin first. A C/intrinsics fixture is worth adding *after* the asm one passes — it then exercises calling-convention spill/fill of V regs, which is a separate concern.

**Recommended sequence:**
1. Program Buffer transfer in `v_regfile_xfer.hpp` (steps 1-3 of the plan).
2. `rt_v_regs.S` Tier-1 fixture, both modes (steps 4-5).
3. Measure wall-clock per handoff. If <100ms, stop here.
4. Only if slow: add DPI probes as a follow-up (separate plan).

Net: lowest-risk path, smallest blast radius, deferred optimization gated by data. Want me to start with step 1?

message.content: what do you mean by "with the asm-fixture default"?

content.text:
I meant: among the two alternates I floated at the end of the plan, default to the asm fixture (`rt_v_regs.S`) rather than a C-intrinsics fixture.

Rephrased: option 1 in the plan has two sub-choices for *how the test program is written* — hand-written assembly using `vle8.v`/`vse8.v` directly, or C with RVV intrinsics (`__riscv_vle8_v_u8m1` etc.). I'm recommending we pick asm.

Reasons asm wins for the *first* RVV fixture:
- Sits in Tier-1 (no shared mmap needed) like the other `rt_*.S` fixtures — fewer moving parts when debugging.
- Direct control: each V reg gets a known pattern with no compiler choices in between.
- No newlib / C-runtime startup, so the V state at the entry trigger is exactly what the asm sets, not whatever the C runtime touched.

A C-intrinsics fixture is worth adding *later* — once the transport works, it gives you coverage of compiler-generated V-reg spill/fill across the boundary, which is a different concern.

So "with the asm-fixture default" = "pick the asm sub-option as the default within option 1." Bad phrasing on my part; sorry for the confusion.

input.description: Probe with vlen=512 + explicit v0 placement

content.text:
`vlen=512` works. Critical findings:

- QEMU needs `-cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512` for RVV (existing tests use `andes-ax45mp`).
- Fixtures need `mstatus.VS=1` in startup before any V insn (otherwise illegal-insn trap).

Quick check: do existing non-V fixtures still pass under `andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512`? If yes, the shell drivers can switch unconditionally; if no, we need per-fixture CPU.

bash -c '
QEMU=/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64
PLUGIN=/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so
ELF=/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf
STATE=$(mktemp)
timeout 8 "$QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$ELF" -plugin "$PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE" 2>&1 | tail -2
echo "state size = $(stat -c %s "$STATE")"
echo "header:"; xxd "$STATE" | head -2
rm -f "$STATE"
'

content.text:
Probe results conclusive. Refined plan:

| Finding | Action |
|---|---|
| Toolchain accepts `-march=rv64gcv`, has `<riscv_vector.h>` | use as-is |
| QEMU `andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512` -> vlenb=64 (matches HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX) | switch shell drivers to this CPU |
| Existing non-V fixtures unchanged under V-enabled CPU (handoff_roundtrip drains 3624 bytes correctly) | safe to switch unconditionally |
| Plugin already captures V state when QEMU advertises it | no plugin change |
| QEMU 2 gdbstub already restores V via existing qemu_handback P packets | no QEMU 2 change |
| vsim still doesn't drain or resume V (the gap I'm filling) | implement PB + scratch RAM |
| Fixture must set `mstatus.VS=1` before V insns | extend runtime/crt0.S |

Let me start with the small no-op step (CPU switch) and confirm regression-clean before getting to the V transport.

toolUseResult.oldString: "$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mp -bios none -nographic \
toolUseResult.newString: "$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -bios none -nographic \
toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end roundtrip:
#   1. QEMU 1 + plugin: drains state on entry trigger.
#   2. vsim: resumes, runs body, drains on exit trigger, exits 200.
#   3. vsim's --qemu-binary path: spawns QEMU 2 with -gdb -S -semihosting,
#      restores GPRs+PC via gdbstub, continues.
#   4. QEMU 2 hits the RISC-V semihosting ebreak-triple, exits with the
#      semihosting exit_code (low 8 bits of a2 in the existing fixture).
#
# Two trigger modes (TRIGGER_MODE env, default csr):
#   csr  - QEMU plugin pattern-matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` to drain on entry;
#          vsim pre-scans the ELF and drains on `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` retire.
#   pc   - orchestrator resolves _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc from the
#          ELF symbol table, passes enter_pc=0x... to the plugin and
#          --hybrid-exit-pc=0x... to vsim. The fixture's csrwi insns are
#          still in the binary (CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor CSR write on
#          andes-ax45mp + the AX45MPV RTL) but the matchers are bypassed.
#
# Fixture-parameterized:
#   FIXTURE       name of .elf under hybrid/test/  (default: handoff_roundtrip)
#   EXPECT_RC     expected vsim process exit code  (default: 3)
#   TRIGGER_MODE  csr | pc                          (default: csr)
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN         path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU        path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN      path to libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF         path to <fixture>.elf  (overrides FIXTURE if set)
#   NM               riscv64-unknown-elf-nm  (default auto-detect)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-3}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77   # CTest skip code when SKIP_RETURN_CODE is set
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> $STATE_QEMU"
# -cpu andes-ax45mp gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl, mxstatus,
# uitb, jvt, mdcause, ...) that crt0/handler from the vsim-demo runtime touch.
# Default rv64 raises illegal-instruction on the very first mcache_ctl write
# in crt0, leaving QEMU spinning in re-trap (mtvec=0). Existing rt_*.S
# fixtures don't touch those CSRs but happily run under the Andes CPU too.
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mp -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, rt_c_file_io)
# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
# -cpu andes-ax45mp gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl, ...)
# touched by the vsim-demo crt0/handler runtime. Default rv64 illegal-traps
# on the very first mcache_ctl write. The asm fixtures (rt_shared_mem.S etc)
# don't use those CSRs but still run cleanly under the Andes CPU.
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine virt,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mp \
  -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, rt_c_file_io)
# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
# -cpu andes-ax45mp gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl, ...)
# touched by the vsim-demo crt0/handler runtime. Default rv64 illegal-traps
# on the very first mcache_ctl write. The asm fixtures (rt_shared_mem.S etc)
# don't use those CSRs but still run cleanly under the Andes CPU.
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine virt,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

file.content:
#=========================================================================
# crt0.S : Entry point for RISC-V user programs
#=========================================================================

#ifndef CRT0_MULTIPLE_HARTS
#define CRT0_MULTIPLE_HARTS 0
#endif

#define mcache_ctl 0x7ca
#define mmisc_ctl 0x7d0

  .text
  .global _start
  .type   _start, @function
_start:
  # Enable icache
  csrsi   mcache_ctl, 0x1

#ifdef ENABLE_DCACHE_COHERENCE
  # Enable dcache coherence management
  li      a0, 1 << 19
  csrs    mcache_ctl, a0
1:
  csrr    a0, mcache_ctl
#if __nds_v5
  bbc     a0, 20, 1b
#else
  srli    a1, a0, 20
  andi    a1, a1, 1
  beqz    a1, 1b
#endif
#endif

  # Enable d-cache and hardware prefetch
  ori     a0, a0, 0x703
  csrw    mcache_ctl, a0

  .weak   _stack
  # Initialize stack pointer (if defined)
  lla     a0, _stack
  beqz    a0, 1f
  mv      sp, a0

#if CRT0_MULTIPLE_HARTS
  csrr    a0, mhartid
  slli    a0, a0, 12
  sub     sp, sp, a0
#endif

  # Initialize global pointer
.option push
.option norelax
1:lla gp, __global_pointer$
.option pop

#ifdef __nds_v5
  csrr    a0, mmsc_cfg
  bbc     a0, 3, 1f
  la      a1, _ITB_BASE_
  csrrw   x0, uitb, a1
1:
#if __riscv_xlen >= 64
  bbc     a0, 52, 1f
#else
  bbc     a0, 31, 1f
  csrr    a0, mmsc_cfg2
  bbc     a0, 20, 1f
#endif
  csrr    a0, mrvarch_cfg
  bbc     a0, 31, 1f
  la      a0, __jvt_base$
  csrw    jvt, a0
#endif
1:

  # Set mstatus.fs to 1 (initial)
  li      a0, (1 << 13)
  csrs    mstatus, a0

  # Set mstatus.vs to 1 (initial)
  li      a0, (1 << 10)
  csrs    mstatus, a0

  # Enable unaligned memory access (mmisc_ctl.UNA)
  li      a0, (1 << 6)
  csrs    mmisc_ctl, a0

#if CRT0_MULTIPLE_HARTS
  csrr    a0, mhartid
  beqz    a0, .Lclear_bss

  li      a1, 0xa55a
.Lother_hart_wait_loop:
  ld      a0, other_hart_boot_flag
  bne     a0, a1, .Lother_hart_wait_loop
  j       .Lmain
#else
  csrr    a0, mhartid
  beqz    a0, .Lclear_bss
  li      a0, 0x01234571
  li      a1, 0xe0080000
  sw      a0, 0(a1)
1:
  j       1b
#endif

  # Clear the bss segment
.Lclear_bss:
  la      a0, _edata
  la      a2, _end
  sub     a2, a2, a0
  li      a1, 0
  call    memset


#if CRT0_MULTIPLE_HARTS
  li      a0, 0xa55a
  lla     a1, other_hart_boot_flag
  sd      a0, 0(a1)
  fence
#endif

  call init_mtvec

.Lmain:
  li      a0, 0
  li      a1, 0
  li      a2, 0
  call    main

  call    _exit

.Lloop:
  j       .Lloop

  .size   _start, .-_start

#if CRT0_MULTIPLE_HARTS
  .data
  .p2align 3
  .global other_hart_boot_flag
  .type   other_hart_boot_flag, @object
other_hart_boot_flag:
  .8byte 0
  .size other_hart_boot_flag, . - other_hart_boot_flag
#endif

                 -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@
