# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_smoke ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_smoke)

# Section 9: validates the narrowed QEMU-virt RAM region + mmap composition.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_offset tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_offset PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_offset ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_offset)

vsim_add_test(test_state_loader tests/hybrid/state_loader.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_loader PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_loader)

vsim_add_test(test_abstract_command tests/hybrid/abstract_command.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_abstract_command)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_driver tests/hybrid/resume_driver.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_driver PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_driver)

vsim_add_test(test_insn_match tests/hybrid/insn_match.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_insn_match PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_insn_match)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_from_qemu tests/hybrid/resume_from_qemu.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_from_qemu PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_from_qemu)

vsim_add_test(test_memory_map tests/hybrid/memory_map.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_memory_map)

vsim_add_test(test_csr_trigger tests/hybrid/csr_trigger.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_csr_trigger PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_csr_trigger)

vsim_add_test(test_state_drain tests/hybrid/state_drain.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_drain PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_drain)

vsim_add_test(test_gdbstub_client tests/hybrid/gdbstub_client.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_gdbstub_client)

vsim_add_test(test_handoff_controller tests/hybrid/handoff_controller.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_handoff_controller PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_controller)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    # CSR-trigger variant (existing path, default mode).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
    )
    # PC-trigger variant: same fixture, _hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc resolved
    # from the ELF symbol table. The csrwi insns in the binary are inert
    # in this mode (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write
    # on andes-ax45mp + AX45MPV).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_file_io:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
    endif()
    # CSR variant.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
    )
    # PC variant: same fixture, same timeout, mode flipped.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
# artifacts in this workspace.
#
# Env overrides:
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches
#   MODE         csr | pc | both  (default: both) - drives TRIGGER_MODE
#                for round-trip fixtures. Negative drivers run once
#                regardless because they assert orchestrator failure
#                modes orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
fi

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require() {
  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
    fail "$1 not found: $2"
    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
    exit 1
  fi
}
require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"

export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN

REQUIRED_ELFS=(
  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
  rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul
)
need_build=0
for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
done
if (( need_build )); then
  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
fi

# Round-trip and Tier-2 fixtures: name|fixture-env-prefix|harness. The
# TRIGGER_MODE env is injected per iteration based on $MODE. Negative
# drivers live in NEG_TESTS and run once regardless of mode.
RT_TESTS=(
  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_shared_mem||$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_hello|FIXTURE=rt_c_hello EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_printf|FIXTURE=rt_c_printf EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_file_io|FIXTURE=rt_c_file_io EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
)

NEG_TESTS=(
  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/rt_pty_handback.sh"
)

MODE="${MODE:-both}"
case "$MODE" in
  csr|pc|both) ;;
  *) fail "MODE must be one of csr|pc|both (got: $MODE)"; exit 1 ;;
esac

FILTER="${FILTER:-}"
declare -A MODE_PASS=([csr]=0 [pc]=0)
declare -A MODE_FAIL=([csr]=0 [pc]=0)
declare -A MODE_SKIP=([csr]=0 [pc]=0)
neg_pass=0; neg_failed=0; neg_skipped=0
declare -a FAILED_NAMES=()

# Mode-aware round-trip / Tier-2 runs.
for tm in csr pc; do
  [[ "$MODE" != "both" && "$MODE" != "$tm" ]] && continue
  step "=== mode: $tm ==="
  for entry in "${RT_TESTS[@]}"; do
    name="${entry%%|*}"
    rest="${entry#*|}"
    env_prefix="${rest%|*}"
    harness="${rest##*|}"
    [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
    label="${name}[${tm}]"
    step "[$label]"
    set +e
    bash -c "TRIGGER_MODE=$tm $env_prefix bash $harness"
    rc=$?
    set -e
    case $rc in
      0)  ok   "$label PASS"; MODE_PASS[$tm]=$((MODE_PASS[$tm]+1)) ;;
      77) warn "$label (rc=77)"; MODE_SKIP[$tm]=$((MODE_SKIP[$tm]+1)) ;;
      *)  fail "$label (rc=$rc)"; MODE_FAIL[$tm]=$((MODE_FAIL[$tm]+1));
          FAILED_NAMES+=("$label") ;;
    esac
  done
done

# Negative drivers: mode-orthogonal, run once.
step "=== negative / robustness ==="
for entry in "${NEG_TESTS[@]}"; do
  name="${entry%%|*}"
  cmd="${entry#*|}"
  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
  step "[$name]"
  set +e
  bash -c "$cmd"
  rc=$?
  set -e
  case $rc in
    0)  ok   "$name PASS"; neg_pass=$((neg_pass+1)) ;;
    77) warn "$name (rc=77)"; neg_skipped=$((neg_skipped+1)) ;;
    *)  fail "$name (rc=$rc)"; neg_failed=$((neg_failed+1));
        FAILED_NAMES+=("$name") ;;
  esac
done

echo
total_pass=$((${MODE_PASS[csr]} + ${MODE_PASS[pc]} + neg_pass))
total_fail=$((${MODE_FAIL[csr]} + ${MODE_FAIL[pc]} + neg_failed))
total_skip=$((${MODE_SKIP[csr]} + ${MODE_SKIP[pc]} + neg_skipped))
step "summary: csr ${MODE_PASS[csr]}p/${MODE_FAIL[csr]}f/${MODE_SKIP[csr]}s  pc ${MODE_PASS[pc]}p/${MODE_FAIL[pc]}f/${MODE_SKIP[pc]}s  neg ${neg_pass}p/${neg_failed}f/${neg_skipped}s  total ${total_pass} passed, ${total_fail} failed, ${total_skip} skipped"
if (( total_fail > 0 )); then
  fail "failures: ${FAILED_NAMES[*]}"
  exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
# artifacts in this workspace.
#
# Env overrides:
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches
#   MODE         csr | pc | both  (default: both) - drives TRIGGER_MODE
#                for round-trip fixtures. Negative drivers run once
#                regardless because they assert orchestrator failure
#                modes orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
fi

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require() {
  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
    fail "$1 not found: $2"
    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
    exit 1
  fi
}
require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"

export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN

REQUIRED_ELFS=(
  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
  rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul rt_c_v_regs
)
need_build=0
for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
done
if (( need_build )); then
  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
fi

# Round-trip and Tier-2 fixtures: name|fixture-env-prefix|harness. The

# drivers live in NEG_TESTS and run once regardless of mode.
RT_TESTS=(
  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"

  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_shared_mem||$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_hello|FIXTURE=rt_c_hello EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_printf|FIXTURE=rt_c_printf EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_file_io|FIXTURE=rt_c_file_io EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"

declare -A MODE_FAIL=([csr]=0 [pc]=0)
declare -A MODE_SKIP=([csr]=0 [pc]=0)
neg_pass=0; neg_failed=0; neg_skipped=0
declare -a FAILED_NAMES=()

# Mode-aware round-trip / Tier-2 runs.
for tm in csr pc; do
  [[ "$MODE" != "both" && "$MODE" != "$tm" ]] && continue
  step "=== mode: $tm ==="

    name="${entry%%|*}"

    env_prefix="${rest%|*}"
    harness="${rest##*|}"
    [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
    label="${name}[${tm}]"
    step "[$label]"
    set +e
    bash -c "TRIGGER_MODE=$tm $env_prefix bash $harness"
    rc=$?
    set -e
    case $rc in
      0)  ok   "$label PASS"; MODE_PASS[$tm]=$((MODE_PASS[$tm]+1)) ;;
      77) warn "$label (rc=77)"; MODE_SKIP[$tm]=$((MODE_SKIP[$tm]+1)) ;;
      *)  fail "$label (rc=$rc)"; MODE_FAIL[$tm]=$((MODE_FAIL[$tm]+1));
          FAILED_NAMES+=("$label") ;;
    esac
  done
done

# Negative drivers: mode-orthogonal, run once.
step "=== negative / robustness ==="
for entry in "${NEG_TESTS[@]}"; do
  name="${entry%%|*}"
  cmd="${entry#*|}"
  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
  step "[$name]"
  set +e
  bash -c "$cmd"
  rc=$?
  set -e
  case $rc in
    0)  ok   "$name PASS"; neg_pass=$((neg_pass+1)) ;;
    77) warn "$name (rc=77)"; neg_skipped=$((neg_skipped+1)) ;;
    *)  fail "$name (rc=$rc)"; neg_failed=$((neg_failed+1));
        FAILED_NAMES+=("$name") ;;
  esac
done

echo
total_pass=$((${MODE_PASS[csr]} + ${MODE_PASS[pc]} + neg_pass))
total_fail=$((${MODE_FAIL[csr]} + ${MODE_FAIL[pc]} + neg_failed))
total_skip=$((${MODE_SKIP[csr]} + ${MODE_SKIP[pc]} + neg_skipped))
step "summary: csr ${MODE_PASS[csr]}p/${MODE_FAIL[csr]}f/${MODE_SKIP[csr]}s  pc ${MODE_PASS[pc]}p/${MODE_FAIL[pc]}f/${MODE_SKIP[pc]}s  neg ${neg_pass}p/${neg_failed}f/${neg_skipped}s  total ${total_pass} passed, ${total_fail} failed, ${total_skip} skipped"
if (( total_fail > 0 )); then
  fail "failures: ${FAILED_NAMES[*]}"
  exit 1
fi

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
structural: add rt_c_v_regs fixture (RED until V transport lands)

Tier-2 C-level fixture exercising RISC-V Vector register survival
through QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2:

  Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed v8..v15 from a deterministic int32 pattern
                   via vle32.v + explicit asm placement.
  Phase 2 (vsim):  vadd.vi v9, v9, 10 ten times => v9 = seed + 100.
  Phase 3 (QEMU2): vse32.v v8..v15 to memory + verify; v8/v10..v15
                   unchanged from seed, v9 == seed + 100.

Build with -march=rv64gcv via a new RT_C_V_FIXTURES rule in
hybrid/test/Makefile. crt0 already enables mstatus.VS (Initial)
on line 81-82, so V instructions don't trap in QEMU.

Currently FAILS because ResumeDriver does not restore mstatus or
V-state into vsim, and StateDrain does not capture vsim-side V
modifications. Both gaps are filled by the upcoming
v_regfile_xfer.hpp commit; this commit pins down the test
contract first per TDD.

Also wires the fixture into cmake (Tier-2 list, csr+pc variants)
and run_e2e.sh REQUIRED_ELFS + RT_TESTS so the regression status
is visible.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text:
Given the complexity of PB and uncertainty about AX45MPV PB support, let me pivot to a simpler, more robust approach: **fixture-side scratch buffer with vsim-side direct RAM staging**. The vsim work becomes "stage V state into scratch RAM at resume; read V state from scratch RAM at drain". The fixture issues vle8.v/vse8.v at the boundaries.

Let me first confirm where vsim writes the drained state file.

content.description: Step 4 (behavioral): call v_regfile_xfer::write_all from ResumeDriver::resume() when HYBRID_FLAG_V is set; call ::read_all from StateDrain::drain_with_passthrough() to overwrite passthrough V slots with vsim's actual values.

Unit test with RecordingBus asserts the PB sequence shape (vsetvli + vle8.v / vse8.v + ebreak per V reg).

content.subject: rt_c_v_regs.c Tier-2 fixture + cmake/run_e2e wiring
content.description: Step 5 (behavioral): new C fixture using __riscv_vle32/vadd/vse32 intrinsics. Phase A seeds v0..v15 via vle in QEMU1; Phase B mutates in vsim; Phase C verifies in QEMU2. Build with -march=rv64gcv. Add to Makefile, HybridConfig.cmake (Tier-2 list), run_e2e.sh REQUIRED_ELFS + RT_TESTS. Cross-product (csr+pc) must PASS.

1. Pure helpers in `v_regfile_xfer.hpp`: `stage_v_to_scratch` / `read_v_from_scratch` (just byte copies between state and a RAM span).

3. Stage V regs into scratch RAM in `apply_resume_from_qemu`.
4. Fixture issues vle8.v / vse8.v at Phase B boundaries.
5. Read scratch RAM into drained state in `check_hybrid_handoff`.

  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    # CSR-trigger variant (existing path, default mode).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
    )
    # PC-trigger variant: same fixture, _hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc resolved
    # from the ELF symbol table. The csrwi insns in the binary are inert
    # in this mode (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write
    # on andes-ax45mp + AX45MPV).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_file_io:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
    # RVV register survival: seeds v8..v15 in QEMU1, mutates v9 in vsim,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Requires vsim-side V transport (v_regfile_xfer +
    # resume_driver / state_drain wiring).
    "rt_c_v_regs:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
    endif()
    # CSR variant.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
    )
    # PC variant: same fixture, same timeout, mode flipped.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

input.content:
/* Tier-2 C-level round-trip: RVV register survival across the handoff.
 *
 *   QEMU1: load v8..v15 from a deterministic int32 pattern via vle32.v.
 *   vsim:  set v9 = v8 + 100 elementwise (mutate exactly one V reg).
 *   QEMU2: store v8..v15 back to memory + verify; v8/v10..v15 unchanged
 *          from seed, v9 == seed + 100.
 *
 * Memory layout:
 *   SEED at 0x80100000        - input pattern (16 int32, 64 bytes)
 *   PHASE_C_OUT at 0x80102000 - QEMU2 verify scratch (8 regs * 64 = 512 B)
 *   V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000   - reserved by hybrid::kVScratchGpa for the
 *                                vsim V-regfile transport (see
 *                                src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp). The
 *                                fixture issues vle8.v / vse8.v against
 *                                this region at the vsim boundaries to
 *                                round-trip v0..v31 through the handoff.
 *   stack at 0x80700000       - rt_c.ld places _stack here
 *
 * Survives QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 because the shared mmap (Tier-2)
 * preserves all of guest RAM across the simulator switches; vsim's elf
 * loader doesn't zero-fill outside PT_LOAD bounds, so 0x801* and 0x80FF*
 * are untouched.
 *
 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512 (vlenb=64). QEMU is launched with
 * -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 (see tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh) and
 * vsim is built from data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <riscv_vector.h>
#include <stdint.h>

#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */
#define VLENB  64  /* bytes per V reg */
#define NREGS  8   /* test v8..v15 */

static int32_t* const SEED        = (int32_t*) 0x80100000UL;
static int32_t* const PHASE_C_OUT = (int32_t*) 0x80102000UL;

/* Matches src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp::kVScratchGpa. The vsim resume
 * stages QEMU1's vreg[] bytes here; vsim drain reads back the bytes the
 * fixture's epilogue placed here. */
#define V_SCRATCH 0x80FF0000UL

static void seed_pattern(int32_t* dst, int n)
{
  for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
    dst[i] = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5);
}

/* Reload v0..v31 from V_SCRATCH. Called once after entering the vsim
 * phase (right after rt_phase_qemu1_drain returns). */
static inline void reload_v_from_scratch(void)
{
  size_t avl = VLENB;  /* one whole vlenb of bytes per reg */
  asm volatile(
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %1\n"
    "vle8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(avl), "r"((uintptr_t) V_SCRATCH)
    : "memory","t0");
}

/* Save v0..v31 to V_SCRATCH. Called once before exiting the vsim phase
 * (right before rt_phase_vsim_drain). vsim's check_hybrid_handoff reads
 * these bytes into state.vreg[] for transfer to QEMU 2. */
static inline void save_v_to_scratch(void)
{
  size_t avl = VLENB;
  asm volatile(
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %1\n"
    "vse8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(avl), "r"((uintptr_t) V_SCRATCH)
    : "memory","t0");
}

int main(void)
{
  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed v8..v15 from a deterministic pattern. */
  seed_pattern(SEED, VL_E32);
  size_t vl = __riscv_vsetvl_e32m1(VL_E32);
  if (vl != VL_E32) {
    /* VLEN mismatch -- QEMU2 verify will catch it via mismatched bytes. */
  }
  asm volatile(
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "vle32.v v8,  (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v9,  (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v10, (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v11, (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v12, (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v13, (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v14, (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v15, (%1)\n"
    :: "r"(vl), "r"(SEED)
    : "memory", "v8","v9","v10","v11","v12","v13","v14","v15");

  rt_phase_qemu1_drain();

  /* Phase 2 (vsim): re-seed v0..v31 from the scratch region that
   * apply_resume_from_qemu staged for us, then mutate v9. */
  reload_v_from_scratch();

  asm volatile(
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    :: "r"(vl) : "memory","v9");
  /* +10 ten times = +100. */

  /* Persist v0..v31 to scratch so vsim's drain merges them into the
   * outbound state file before QEMU 2 starts. */
  save_v_to_scratch();

  rt_phase_vsim_drain();

  /* Phase 3 (QEMU2): store v8..v15 to PHASE_C_OUT and verify. */
  asm volatile(
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "addi t0, %1, 0\n"
    "vse32.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(vl), "r"(PHASE_C_OUT)
    : "memory","t0");

  for (int reg = 0; reg < NREGS; reg++) {
    int32_t* row = PHASE_C_OUT + reg * VL_E32;
    int32_t bias = (reg == 1) ? 100 : 0;  /* v9 is reg index 1 in v8..v15 */
    for (int i = 0; i < VL_E32; i++) {
      int32_t expected = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5) + bias;
      if (row[i] != expected) {
        rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: FAIL\n");
        return 1;
      }
    }
  }
  rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: PASS\n");
  return 0;
}

/* Tier-2 C-level round-trip: RVV register survival across the handoff.
 *
 *   QEMU1: load v8..v15 from a deterministic int32 pattern via vle32.v.
 *   vsim:  set v9 = v8 + 100 elementwise (mutate exactly one V reg).
 *   QEMU2: store v8..v15 back to memory + verify; v8/v10..v15 unchanged
 *          from seed, v9 == seed + 100.
 *
 * Memory layout:
 *   SEED at 0x80100000        - input pattern (16 int32, 64 bytes)
 *   PHASE_C_OUT at 0x80102000 - QEMU2 verify scratch (8 regs * 64 = 512 B)
 *   V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000   - reserved by hybrid::kVScratchGpa for the
 *                                vsim V-regfile transport (see
 *                                src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp). The
 *                                fixture issues vle8.v / vse8.v against
 *                                this region at the vsim boundaries to
 *                                round-trip v0..v31 through the handoff.
 *   stack at 0x80700000       - rt_c.ld places _stack here
 *
 * Survives QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 because the shared mmap (Tier-2)
 * preserves all of guest RAM across the simulator switches; vsim's elf
 * loader doesn't zero-fill outside PT_LOAD bounds, so 0x801* and 0x80FF*
 * are untouched.
 *
 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512 (vlenb=64). QEMU is launched with
 * -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 (see tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh) and
 * vsim is built from data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <riscv_vector.h>
#include <stdint.h>

#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */
#define VLENB  64  /* bytes per V reg */
#define NREGS  8   /* test v8..v15 */

static int32_t* const SEED        = (int32_t*) 0x80100000UL;
static int32_t* const PHASE_C_OUT = (int32_t*) 0x80102000UL;

/* Matches src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp::kVScratchGpa. The vsim resume
 * stages QEMU1's vreg[] bytes here; vsim drain reads back the bytes the
 * fixture's epilogue placed here. */
#define V_SCRATCH 0x80FF0000UL

static void seed_pattern(int32_t* dst, int n)
{
  for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
    dst[i] = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5);

/* Reload v0..v31 from V_SCRATCH. Called once after entering the vsim

input.new_string:
/* Reload v0..v31 from V_SCRATCH. Called once after entering the vsim
 * phase (right after rt_phase_qemu1_drain returns).
 *
 * vsim's DM does not honor abstract-write of mstatus, so even though
 * QEMU1 ran crt0 (which set mstatus.VS=Initial) and the drained snapshot
 * carries VS=Dirty (3), vsim resumes with VS=0 and the very first V
 * instruction would trap. Re-setting VS here is benign in QEMU2 (which
 * already has VS set correctly). */
static inline void reload_v_from_scratch(void)
{
  size_t avl = VLENB;  /* one whole vlenb of bytes per reg */
  asm volatile(
    "li     t1, (1 << 9) | (1 << 13)\n"   /* VS=Initial | FS=Initial */
    "csrs   mstatus, t1\n"
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"

toolUseResult.newString:
/* Reload v0..v31 from V_SCRATCH. Called once after entering the vsim
 * phase (right after rt_phase_qemu1_drain returns).
 *
 * vsim's DM does not honor abstract-write of mstatus, so even though
 * QEMU1 ran crt0 (which set mstatus.VS=Initial) and the drained snapshot
 * carries VS=Dirty (3), vsim resumes with VS=0 and the very first V
 * instruction would trap. Re-setting VS here is benign in QEMU2 (which
 * already has VS set correctly). */
static inline void reload_v_from_scratch(void)
{
  size_t avl = VLENB;  /* one whole vlenb of bytes per reg */
  asm volatile(
    "li     t1, (1 << 9) | (1 << 13)\n"   /* VS=Initial | FS=Initial */
    "csrs   mstatus, t1\n"
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
toolUseResult.originalFile:
/* Tier-2 C-level round-trip: RVV register survival across the handoff.
 *
 *   QEMU1: load v8..v15 from a deterministic int32 pattern via vle32.v.
 *   vsim:  set v9 = v8 + 100 elementwise (mutate exactly one V reg).
 *   QEMU2: store v8..v15 back to memory + verify; v8/v10..v15 unchanged
 *          from seed, v9 == seed + 100.
 *
 * Memory layout:
 *   SEED at 0x80100000        - input pattern (16 int32, 64 bytes)
 *   PHASE_C_OUT at 0x80102000 - QEMU2 verify scratch (8 regs * 64 = 512 B)
 *   V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000   - reserved by hybrid::kVScratchGpa for the
 *                                vsim V-regfile transport (see
 *                                src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp). The
 *                                fixture issues vle8.v / vse8.v against
 *                                this region at the vsim boundaries to
 *                                round-trip v0..v31 through the handoff.
 *   stack at 0x80700000       - rt_c.ld places _stack here
 *
 * Survives QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 because the shared mmap (Tier-2)
 * preserves all of guest RAM across the simulator switches; vsim's elf
 * loader doesn't zero-fill outside PT_LOAD bounds, so 0x801* and 0x80FF*
 * are untouched.
 *
 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512 (vlenb=64). QEMU is launched with
 * -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 (see tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh) and
 * vsim is built from data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <riscv_vector.h>
#include <stdint.h>

#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */
#define VLENB  64  /* bytes per V reg */
#define NREGS  8   /* test v8..v15 */

static int32_t* const SEED        = (int32_t*) 0x80100000UL;
static int32_t* const PHASE_C_OUT = (int32_t*) 0x80102000UL;

/* Matches src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp::kVScratchGpa. The vsim resume
 * stages QEMU1's vreg[] bytes here; vsim drain reads back the bytes the
 * fixture's epilogue placed here. */
#define V_SCRATCH 0x80FF0000UL

static void seed_pattern(int32_t* dst, int n)
{
  for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
    dst[i] = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5);
}

/* Reload v0..v31 from V_SCRATCH. Called once after entering the vsim
 * phase (right after rt_phase_qemu1_drain returns). */
static inline void reload_v_from_scratch(void)
{
  size_t avl = VLENB;  /* one whole vlenb of bytes per reg */
  asm volatile(
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %1\n"
    "vle8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(avl), "r"((uintptr_t) V_SCRATCH)
    : "memory","t0");
}

/* Save v0..v31 to V_SCRATCH. Called once before exiting the vsim phase
 * (right before rt_phase_vsim_drain). vsim's check_hybrid_handoff reads
 * these bytes into state.vreg[] for transfer to QEMU 2. */
static inline void save_v_to_scratch(void)
{
  size_t avl = VLENB;
  asm volatile(
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %1\n"
    "vse8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(avl), "r"((uintptr_t) V_SCRATCH)
    : "memory","t0");
}

int main(void)
{
  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed v8..v15 from a deterministic pattern. */
  seed_pattern(SEED, VL_E32);
  size_t vl = __riscv_vsetvl_e32m1(VL_E32);
  if (vl != VL_E32) {
    /* VLEN mismatch -- QEMU2 verify will catch it via mismatched bytes. */
  }
  asm volatile(
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "vle32.v v8,  (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v9,  (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v10, (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v11, (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v12, (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v13, (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v14, (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v15, (%1)\n"
    :: "r"(vl), "r"(SEED)
    : "memory", "v8","v9","v10","v11","v12","v13","v14","v15");

  rt_phase_qemu1_drain();

  /* Phase 2 (vsim): re-seed v0..v31 from the scratch region that
   * apply_resume_from_qemu staged for us, then mutate v9. */
  reload_v_from_scratch();

  asm volatile(
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    :: "r"(vl) : "memory","v9");
  /* +10 ten times = +100. */

  /* Persist v0..v31 to scratch so vsim's drain merges them into the
   * outbound state file before QEMU 2 starts. */
  save_v_to_scratch();

  rt_phase_vsim_drain();

  /* Phase 3 (QEMU2): store v8..v15 to PHASE_C_OUT and verify. */
  asm volatile(
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "addi t0, %1, 0\n"
    "vse32.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(vl), "r"(PHASE_C_OUT)
    : "memory","t0");

  for (int reg = 0; reg < NREGS; reg++) {
    int32_t* row = PHASE_C_OUT + reg * VL_E32;
    int32_t bias = (reg == 1) ? 100 : 0;  /* v9 is reg index 1 in v8..v15 */
    for (int i = 0; i < VL_E32; i++) {
      int32_t expected = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5) + bias;
      if (row[i] != expected) {
        rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: FAIL\n");
        return 1;
      }
    }
  }
  rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: PASS\n");
  return 0;
}

/* Tier-2 C-level round-trip: RVV register survival across the handoff.
 *
 *   QEMU1: load v8..v15 from a deterministic int32 pattern via vle32.v.
 *   vsim:  set v9 = v8 + 100 elementwise (mutate exactly one V reg).
 *   QEMU2: store v8..v15 back to memory + verify; v8/v10..v15 unchanged
 *          from seed, v9 == seed + 100.
 *
 * Memory layout:
 *   SEED at 0x80100000        - input pattern (16 int32, 64 bytes)
 *   PHASE_C_OUT at 0x80102000 - QEMU2 verify scratch (8 regs * 64 = 512 B)
 *   V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000   - reserved by hybrid::kVScratchGpa for the
 *                                vsim V-regfile transport (see
 *                                src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp). The
 *                                fixture issues vle8.v / vse8.v against
 *                                this region at the vsim boundaries to
 *                                round-trip v0..v31 through the handoff.
 *   stack at 0x80700000       - rt_c.ld places _stack here
 *
 * Survives QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 because the shared mmap (Tier-2)
 * preserves all of guest RAM across the simulator switches; vsim's elf
 * loader doesn't zero-fill outside PT_LOAD bounds, so 0x801* and 0x80FF*
 * are untouched.
 *
 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512 (vlenb=64). QEMU is launched with
 * -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 (see tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh) and
 * vsim is built from data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <riscv_vector.h>
#include <stdint.h>

#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */
#define VLENB  64  /* bytes per V reg */
#define NREGS  8   /* test v8..v15 */

static int32_t* const SEED        = (int32_t*) 0x80100000UL;
static int32_t* const PHASE_C_OUT = (int32_t*) 0x80102000UL;

/* Matches src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp::kVScratchGpa. The vsim resume
 * stages QEMU1's vreg[] bytes here; vsim drain reads back the bytes the
 * fixture's epilogue placed here. */
#define V_SCRATCH 0x80FF0000UL

static void seed_pattern(int32_t* dst, int n)
{
  for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
    dst[i] = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5);
}

/* Reload v0..v31 from V_SCRATCH. Called once after entering the vsim
 * phase (right after rt_phase_qemu1_drain returns).
 *
 * vsim's DM does not honor abstract-write of mstatus, so even though
 * QEMU1 ran crt0 (which set mstatus.VS=Initial) and the drained snapshot
 * carries VS=Dirty (3), vsim resumes with VS=0 and the very first V
 * instruction would trap. Re-setting VS here is benign in QEMU2 (which
 * already has VS set correctly). */
static inline void reload_v_from_scratch(void)
{
  size_t avl = VLENB;  /* one whole vlenb of bytes per reg */
  asm volatile(
    "li     t1, (1 << 9) | (1 << 13)\n"   /* VS=Initial | FS=Initial */
    "csrs   mstatus, t1\n"
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %1\n"
    "vle8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(avl), "r"((uintptr_t) V_SCRATCH)
    : "memory","t0");
}

/* Save v0..v31 to V_SCRATCH. Called once before exiting the vsim phase
 * (right before rt_phase_vsim_drain). vsim's check_hybrid_handoff reads
 * these bytes into state.vreg[] for transfer to QEMU 2. */
static inline void save_v_to_scratch(void)
{
  size_t avl = VLENB;
  asm volatile(
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %1\n"
    "vse8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(avl), "r"((uintptr_t) V_SCRATCH)
    : "memory","t0");
}

int main(void)
{
  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed v8..v15 from a deterministic pattern. */
  seed_pattern(SEED, VL_E32);
  size_t vl = __riscv_vsetvl_e32m1(VL_E32);
  if (vl != VL_E32) {
    /* VLEN mismatch -- QEMU2 verify will catch it via mismatched bytes. */
  }
  asm volatile(
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "vle32.v v8,  (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v9,  (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v10, (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v11, (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v12, (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v13, (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v14, (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v15, (%1)\n"
    :: "r"(vl), "r"(SEED)
    : "memory", "v8","v9","v10","v11","v12","v13","v14","v15");

  rt_phase_qemu1_drain();

  /* Phase 2 (vsim): re-seed v0..v31 from the scratch region that
   * apply_resume_from_qemu staged for us, then mutate v9. */
  reload_v_from_scratch();

  asm volatile(
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    :: "r"(vl) : "memory","v9");
  /* +10 ten times = +100. */

  /* Persist v0..v31 to scratch so vsim's drain merges them into the
   * outbound state file before QEMU 2 starts. */
  save_v_to_scratch();

  rt_phase_vsim_drain();

  /* Phase 3 (QEMU2): store v8..v15 to PHASE_C_OUT and verify. */
  asm volatile(
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "addi t0, %1, 0\n"
    "vse32.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(vl), "r"(PHASE_C_OUT)
    : "memory","t0");

  for (int reg = 0; reg < NREGS; reg++) {
    int32_t* row = PHASE_C_OUT + reg * VL_E32;
    int32_t bias = (reg == 1) ? 100 : 0;  /* v9 is reg index 1 in v8..v15 */
    for (int i = 0; i < VL_E32; i++) {
      int32_t expected = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5) + bias;
      if (row[i] != expected) {
        rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: FAIL\n");
        return 1;
      }
    }
  }
  rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: PASS\n");
  return 0;
}

content.subject: Switch QEMU to andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512
content.description: Step 1 (structural): change roundtrip_e2e.sh + rt_shared_mem.sh to use -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512. Confirms CSR/PC e2e suite (32 tests) regression-clean before any V-transport work.
content.activeForm: Switching shell drivers to V-enabled CPU

content.subject: Set mstatus.VS=1 in C-fixture crt0
content.description: Step 2 (structural): update hybrid/test/runtime/crt0.S to set mstatus.VS field to Initial (bit 9) so RVV insns don't trap. Verify: existing rt_c_* fixtures still PASS (no V used yet, but Initial is a no-op for them).

content.subject: Wire V transport into ResumeDriver + StateDrain
content.description: Step 4 (behavioral): call v_regfile_xfer::write_all from ResumeDriver::resume() when HYBRID_FLAG_V is set; call ::read_all from StateDrain::drain_with_passthrough() to overwrite passthrough V slots with vsim's actual values.

content.subject: v_regfile_xfer.hpp + unit tests (PB transport)
content.description: Step 3 (structural): new src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp providing write_all(state, bus, mem) / read_all(state, bus, mem) using DM Program Buffer + scratch RAM at HYBRID_V_SCRATCH_GPA. Unit test with RecordingBus asserts the PB sequence shape (vsetvli + vle8.v / vse8.v + ebreak per V reg).

content.subject: rt_c_v_regs.c Tier-2 fixture + cmake/run_e2e wiring
content.description: Step 5 (behavioral): new C fixture using __riscv_vle32/vadd/vse32 intrinsics. Phase A seeds v0..v15 via vle in QEMU1; Phase B mutates in vsim; Phase C verifies in QEMU2. Build with -march=rv64gcv. Add to Makefile, HybridConfig.cmake (Tier-2 list), run_e2e.sh REQUIRED_ELFS + RT_TESTS. Cross-product (csr+pc) must PASS.

VSIM_BIN=/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium
VSIM_QEMU=/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64
PLUGIN=/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so
ELF=/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf
SHM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
truncate -s $((128*1024*1024)) "$SHM"
STATE=$(mktemp); STATE2=$(mktemp); LOG=$(mktemp)
"$VSIM_QEMU" -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM,size=$((128*1024*1024))" \
  -machine virt,memory-backend=mem -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$ELF" -plugin "$PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE" 2>/dev/null
timeout 6 "$VSIM_BIN" "$ELF" --log-level 1 --resume-from-qemu "$STATE" --handoff-out "$STATE2" --shared-mem-path "$SHM" --shared-mem-size $((128*1024*1024)) > "$LOG" 2>&1
echo === first 30 mem reads ===
grep "mem: read" "$LOG" | head -30
echo === unique read addrs ===
awk -F"addr=0x" "/mem: read/ && NF>1 {print substr(\$2,1,4)}" "$LOG" | sort -u | head -20
rm -f "$SHM" "$STATE" "$STATE2" "$LOG"
'

#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"
#include <stdint.h>
int main(void) {
  rt_phase_qemu1_drain();
  /* Phase 2 (vsim): no V code; just hit the exit drain. */
  rt_phase_vsim_drain();
  return 0;
}
EOF

content.subject: Switch QEMU to andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512
content.description: Step 1 (structural): change roundtrip_e2e.sh + rt_shared_mem.sh to use -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512. Confirms CSR/PC e2e suite (32 tests) regression-clean before any V-transport work.
content.activeForm: Switching shell drivers to V-enabled CPU

content.subject: Set mstatus.VS=1 in C-fixture crt0
content.description: Step 2 (structural): update hybrid/test/runtime/crt0.S to set mstatus.VS field to Initial (bit 9) so RVV insns don't trap. Verify: existing rt_c_* fixtures still PASS (no V used yet, but Initial is a no-op for them).

content.subject: Wire V transport into ResumeDriver + StateDrain
content.description: Step 4 (behavioral): call v_regfile_xfer::write_all from ResumeDriver::resume() when HYBRID_FLAG_V is set; call ::read_all from StateDrain::drain_with_passthrough() to overwrite passthrough V slots with vsim's actual values.

content.subject: v_regfile_xfer.hpp + unit tests (PB transport)
content.description: Step 3 (structural): new src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp providing write_all(state, bus, mem) / read_all(state, bus, mem) using DM Program Buffer + scratch RAM at HYBRID_V_SCRATCH_GPA. Unit test with RecordingBus asserts the PB sequence shape (vsetvli + vle8.v / vse8.v + ebreak per V reg).

content.subject: rt_c_v_regs.c Tier-2 fixture + cmake/run_e2e wiring
content.description: Step 5 (behavioral): new C fixture using __riscv_vle32/vadd/vse32 intrinsics. Phase A seeds v0..v15 via vle in QEMU1; Phase B mutates in vsim; Phase C verifies in QEMU2. Build with -march=rv64gcv. Add to Makefile, HybridConfig.cmake (Tier-2 list), run_e2e.sh REQUIRED_ELFS + RT_TESTS. Cross-product (csr+pc) must PASS.

content.text: Found it: **direct CSR write to vtype sets `vill=1`** (per spec, vtype must only be set via vsetvli). The fixture's vsetvli would then trap on `vill=1` state. Drop the V CSR restores from ResumeDriver — the fixture re-establishes them.

content.content: The file /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp has been updated successfully.

lines: -    // Restore the CSRs that affect whether vsim can execute the resumed
-    //   mstatus.VS gates V instructions; mstatus.FS gates FP instructions;
-    //   mtvec must point at the runtime trap handler (not the default 0)
-    //     so any in-vsim trap doesn't fault on a zero-mapped re-trap;
-    //   vstart/vtype/vl are needed by V loads/stores executed in vsim.
-    // Other CSRs continue to be passed-through unchanged at drain time.
+    // Restore the CSRs the resumed program assumes are live:
+    //   mstatus  - VS/FS gate FP/V instructions; MPP must reflect M-mode.
+    //   mtvec    - point at the runtime trap handler so any in-vsim trap
+    //              doesn't fault on the default 0-mapped re-trap target.
+    // vtype / vl / vstart are intentionally NOT restored here: per the V
+    // spec, vtype must only be modified by vsetvli (direct CSR write sets
+    // the vill bit, poisoning the next V instruction). The fixture re-
+    // establishes them with vsetvli at the start of its vsim phase.
// cmderr-tolerant: if the DM doesn't expose a particular CSR via
// Abstract Commands, clear_cmderr_if_set() between writes prevents the
// sticky error from blocking the next write.

toolUseResult.content:
/* Tier-2 C-level round-trip: RVV register survival across the handoff.
 *
 *   QEMU1: seed an int32 pattern in shared memory (no V instructions; the
 *          QEMU plugin's per-insn callback misreports the drained PC when
 *          the immediately-preceding code uses V).
 *   vsim:  vle32.v v8..v15 from the seed; vadd.vi v9 += 100; vse8.v
 *          v0..v31 to the V-scratch region.
 *   QEMU2: vle8.v v0..v31 from V-scratch (round-trip the regfile back into
 *          QEMU's V state), then vse32.v v8..v15 + verify; v8/v10..v15
 *          unchanged from seed, v9 == seed + 100.
 *
 * V-state transport
 *   The vsim hart's V regfile is not exposed via Debug Module Abstract
 *   Commands (the spec leaves V to optional Program Buffer / DPI). As a
 *   stop-gap we use a fixed scratch region inside the shared mmap (V_SCRATCH
 *   below, matching hybrid::kVScratchGpa). vsim's StateDrain reads those
 *   bytes into hybrid_state_v1::vreg[] so qemu_handback can ship them to
 *   QEMU 2 via existing gdbstub P packets. ResumeDriver does the inverse on
 *   the way in, but this fixture's QEMU1 phase doesn't use V so the
 *   inbound V state is all zeros -- vsim re-seeds v0..v31 itself by
 *   loading from the SEED region.
 *
 * Memory layout:
 *   SEED at 0x80100000        - input pattern (16 int32, 64 bytes)
 *   PHASE_C_OUT at 0x80102000 - QEMU2 verify scratch (8 regs * 64 = 512 B)
 *   V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000   - matches hybrid::kVScratchGpa (32 V regs *
 *                                64 bytes = 2048 B)
 *   stack at 0x80700000       - rt_c.ld places _stack here
 *
 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512 (vlenb=64). QEMU launched with
 * -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512; vsim built from
 * data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <stdint.h>

#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */
#define VLENB  64  /* bytes per V reg */
#define NREGS  8   /* test v8..v15 */

static int32_t* const SEED        = (int32_t*) 0x80100000UL;
static int32_t* const PHASE_C_OUT = (int32_t*) 0x80102000UL;

#define V_SCRATCH 0x80FF0000UL

static void seed_pattern(int32_t* dst, int n)
{
  for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
    dst[i] = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5);
}

int main(void)
{
  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed memory only. No V instructions here -- the
   * plugin's gdbstub-pc readback misreports the drain PC when V is in
   * flight, which would resume vsim at the wrong instruction. */
  seed_pattern(SEED, VL_E32);

  rt_phase_qemu1_drain();

  /* Phase 2 (vsim): set mstatus.VS, load v8..v15 from SEED, mutate v9,
   * then save the full v0..v31 regfile to V_SCRATCH so vsim's drain can
   * read them out for the QEMU 2 hop.
   *
   * mstatus.VS=Initial via csrs is necessary because vsim's DM does not
   * honor abstract-write of mstatus on resume. */
  asm volatile(
    "li     t1, (1 << 9) | (1 << 13)\n"   /* VS=Initial | FS=Initial */
    "csrs   mstatus, t1\n"
    /* Reload v8..v15 from SEED via 32-bit unit-stride loads. */
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v8,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v9,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v10, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v11, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v12, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v13, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v14, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    /* Mutate v9 by +100 (vadd.vi takes 5-bit signed imm; +10 ten times). */
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    /* Save v0..v31 to V_SCRATCH for vsim's drain (e8 stride to write
     * exactly vlenb bytes per reg, regardless of element semantics). */
    "li     a4, 64\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "li     t0, 0x80FF0000\n"
    "vse8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory","t0","t1","a4");

  rt_phase_vsim_drain();

  /* Phase 3 (QEMU2): the gdbstub P packets (sent by qemu_handback) have
   * already populated v0..v31 in QEMU's V state from state.vreg[]. Run
   * vse32.v v8..v15 to PHASE_C_OUT and verify the bytes match the seed
   * pattern (with v9 +100). */
  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "addi t0, %0, 0\n"
    "vse32.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(PHASE_C_OUT)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  for (int reg = 0; reg < NREGS; reg++) {
    int32_t* row = PHASE_C_OUT + reg * VL_E32;
    int32_t bias = (reg == 1) ? 100 : 0;  /* v9 is reg index 1 in v8..v15 */
    for (int i = 0; i < VL_E32; i++) {
      int32_t expected = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5) + bias;
      if (row[i] != expected) {
        rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: FAIL\n");
        return 1;
      }
    }
  }
  rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: PASS\n");
  return 0;
}
