toolUseResult.originalFile:
/* Tier-2 C-level round-trip: RVV register survival across the handoff.
 *
 *   QEMU1: load v8..v15 from a deterministic int32 pattern via vle32.v.
 *   vsim:  set v9 = v8 + 100 elementwise (mutate exactly one V reg).
 *   QEMU2: store v8..v15 back to memory + verify; v8/v10..v15 unchanged
 *          from seed, v9 == seed + 100.
 *
 * Memory layout:
 *   SEED at 0x80100000        - input pattern (16 int32, 64 bytes)
 *   PHASE_C_OUT at 0x80102000 - QEMU2 verify scratch (8 regs * 64 = 512 B)
 *   V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000   - reserved by hybrid::kVScratchGpa for the
 *                                vsim V-regfile transport (see
 *                                src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp). The
 *                                fixture issues vle8.v / vse8.v against
 *                                this region at the vsim boundaries to
 *                                round-trip v0..v31 through the handoff.
 *   stack at 0x80700000       - rt_c.ld places _stack here
 *
 * Survives QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 because the shared mmap (Tier-2)
 * preserves all of guest RAM across the simulator switches; vsim's elf
 * loader doesn't zero-fill outside PT_LOAD bounds, so 0x801* and 0x80FF*
 * are untouched.
 *
 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512 (vlenb=64). QEMU is launched with
 * -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 (see tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh) and
 * vsim is built from data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <riscv_vector.h>
#include <stdint.h>

#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */
#define VLENB  64  /* bytes per V reg */
#define NREGS  8   /* test v8..v15 */

static int32_t* const SEED        = (int32_t*) 0x80100000UL;
static int32_t* const PHASE_C_OUT = (int32_t*) 0x80102000UL;

/* Matches src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp::kVScratchGpa. The vsim resume
 * stages QEMU1's vreg[] bytes here; vsim drain reads back the bytes the
 * fixture's epilogue placed here. */
#define V_SCRATCH 0x80FF0000UL

static void seed_pattern(int32_t* dst, int n)
{
  for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
    dst[i] = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5);
}

/* Reload v0..v31 from V_SCRATCH. Called once after entering the vsim
 * phase (right after rt_phase_qemu1_drain returns).
 *
 * vsim's DM does not honor abstract-write of mstatus, so even though
 * QEMU1 ran crt0 (which set mstatus.VS=Initial) and the drained snapshot
 * carries VS=Dirty (3), vsim resumes with VS=0 and the very first V
 * instruction would trap. Re-setting VS here is benign in QEMU2 (which
 * already has VS set correctly). */
static inline void reload_v_from_scratch(void)
{
  size_t avl = VLENB;  /* one whole vlenb of bytes per reg */
  asm volatile(
    "li     t1, (1 << 9) | (1 << 13)\n"   /* VS=Initial | FS=Initial */
    "csrs   mstatus, t1\n"
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %1\n"
    "vle8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vle8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(avl), "r"((uintptr_t) V_SCRATCH)
    : "memory","t0","t1");
}

/* Save v0..v31 to V_SCRATCH. Called once before exiting the vsim phase
 * (right before rt_phase_vsim_drain). vsim's check_hybrid_handoff reads
 * these bytes into state.vreg[] for transfer to QEMU 2. */
static inline void save_v_to_scratch(void)
{
  size_t avl = VLENB;
  asm volatile(
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %1\n"
    "vse8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(avl), "r"((uintptr_t) V_SCRATCH)
    : "memory","t0");
}

int main(void)
{
  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed v8..v15 from a deterministic pattern. */
  seed_pattern(SEED, VL_E32);
  size_t vl = __riscv_vsetvl_e32m1(VL_E32);
  if (vl != VL_E32) {
    /* VLEN mismatch -- QEMU2 verify will catch it via mismatched bytes. */
  }
  asm volatile(
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "vle32.v v8,  (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v9,  (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v10, (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v11, (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v12, (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v13, (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v14, (%1)\n"
    "vle32.v v15, (%1)\n"
    :: "r"(vl), "r"(SEED)
    : "memory", "v8","v9","v10","v11","v12","v13","v14","v15");

  rt_phase_qemu1_drain();

  /* Phase 2 (vsim): re-seed v0..v31 from the scratch region that
   * apply_resume_from_qemu staged for us, then mutate v9. */
  reload_v_from_scratch();

  asm volatile(
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    :: "r"(vl) : "memory","v9");
  /* +10 ten times = +100. */

  /* Persist v0..v31 to scratch so vsim's drain merges them into the
   * outbound state file before QEMU 2 starts. */
  save_v_to_scratch();

  rt_phase_vsim_drain();

  /* Phase 3 (QEMU2): store v8..v15 to PHASE_C_OUT and verify. */
  asm volatile(
    "vsetvli zero, %0, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "addi t0, %1, 0\n"
    "vse32.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(vl), "r"(PHASE_C_OUT)
    : "memory","t0");

  for (int reg = 0; reg < NREGS; reg++) {
    int32_t* row = PHASE_C_OUT + reg * VL_E32;
    int32_t bias = (reg == 1) ? 100 : 0;  /* v9 is reg index 1 in v8..v15 */
    for (int i = 0; i < VL_E32; i++) {
      int32_t expected = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5) + bias;
      if (row[i] != expected) {
        rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: FAIL\n");
        return 1;
      }
    }
  }
  rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: PASS\n");
  return 0;
}

content.text: Bug uncovered: plugin's reported PC for V-using fixtures is wrong (captures 0x80000f52 = first vle32.v, not csrwi at 0x80000f62). Likely a QEMU plugin gdbstub-pc timing issue with vector instructions. Workaround: do V work entirely in the vsim phase, not in QEMU 1. The test goal (V regs round-trip vsim->QEMU2) doesn't require QEMU 1 to use V.

input.content:
/* Tier-2 C-level round-trip: RVV register survival across the handoff.
 *
 *   QEMU1: seed an int32 pattern in shared memory (no V instructions; the
 *          QEMU plugin's per-insn callback misreports the drained PC when
 *          the immediately-preceding code uses V).
 *   vsim:  vle32.v v8..v15 from the seed; vadd.vi v9 += 100; vse8.v
 *          v0..v31 to the V-scratch region.
 *   QEMU2: vle8.v v0..v31 from V-scratch (round-trip the regfile back into
 *          QEMU's V state), then vse32.v v8..v15 + verify; v8/v10..v15
 *          unchanged from seed, v9 == seed + 100.
 *
 * V-state transport
 *   The vsim hart's V regfile is not exposed via Debug Module Abstract
 *   Commands (the spec leaves V to optional Program Buffer / DPI). As a
 *   stop-gap we use a fixed scratch region inside the shared mmap (V_SCRATCH
 *   below, matching hybrid::kVScratchGpa). vsim's StateDrain reads those
 *   bytes into hybrid_state_v1::vreg[] so qemu_handback can ship them to
 *   QEMU 2 via existing gdbstub P packets. ResumeDriver does the inverse on
 *   the way in, but this fixture's QEMU1 phase doesn't use V so the
 *   inbound V state is all zeros -- vsim re-seeds v0..v31 itself by
 *   loading from the SEED region.
 *
 * Memory layout:
 *   SEED at 0x80100000        - input pattern (16 int32, 64 bytes)
 *   PHASE_C_OUT at 0x80102000 - QEMU2 verify scratch (8 regs * 64 = 512 B)
 *   V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000   - matches hybrid::kVScratchGpa (32 V regs *
 *                                64 bytes = 2048 B)
 *   stack at 0x80700000       - rt_c.ld places _stack here
 *
 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512 (vlenb=64). QEMU launched with
 * -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512; vsim built from
 * data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <stdint.h>

#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */
#define VLENB  64  /* bytes per V reg */
#define NREGS  8   /* test v8..v15 */

static int32_t* const SEED        = (int32_t*) 0x80100000UL;
static int32_t* const PHASE_C_OUT = (int32_t*) 0x80102000UL;

#define V_SCRATCH 0x80FF0000UL

static void seed_pattern(int32_t* dst, int n)
{
  for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
    dst[i] = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5);
}

int main(void)
{
  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed memory only. No V instructions here -- the
   * plugin's gdbstub-pc readback misreports the drain PC when V is in
   * flight, which would resume vsim at the wrong instruction. */
  seed_pattern(SEED, VL_E32);

  rt_phase_qemu1_drain();

  /* Phase 2 (vsim): set mstatus.VS, load v8..v15 from SEED, mutate v9,
   * then save the full v0..v31 regfile to V_SCRATCH so vsim's drain can
   * read them out for the QEMU 2 hop.
   *
   * mstatus.VS=Initial via csrs is necessary because vsim's DM does not
   * honor abstract-write of mstatus on resume. */
  asm volatile(
    "li     t1, (1 << 9) | (1 << 13)\n"   /* VS=Initial | FS=Initial */
    "csrs   mstatus, t1\n"
    /* Reload v8..v15 from SEED via 32-bit unit-stride loads. */
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v8,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v9,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v10, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v11, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v12, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v13, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v14, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    /* Mutate v9 by +100 (vadd.vi takes 5-bit signed imm; +10 ten times). */
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    /* Save v0..v31 to V_SCRATCH for vsim's drain (e8 stride to write
     * exactly vlenb bytes per reg, regardless of element semantics). */
    "li     a4, 64\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "li     t0, 0x80FF0000\n"
    "vse8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory","t0","t1","a4");

  rt_phase_vsim_drain();

  /* Phase 3 (QEMU2): the gdbstub P packets (sent by qemu_handback) have
   * already populated v0..v31 in QEMU's V state from state.vreg[]. Run
   * vse32.v v8..v15 to PHASE_C_OUT and verify the bytes match the seed
   * pattern (with v9 +100). */
  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "addi t0, %0, 0\n"
    "vse32.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(PHASE_C_OUT)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  for (int reg = 0; reg < NREGS; reg++) {
    int32_t* row = PHASE_C_OUT + reg * VL_E32;
    int32_t bias = (reg == 1) ? 100 : 0;  /* v9 is reg index 1 in v8..v15 */
    for (int i = 0; i < VL_E32; i++) {
      int32_t expected = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5) + bias;
      if (row[i] != expected) {
        rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: FAIL\n");
        return 1;
      }
    }
  }
  rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: PASS\n");
  return 0;
}

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/* Tier-2 C-level round-trip: RVV register survival across the handoff.
 *
 *   QEMU1: seed an int32 pattern in shared memory (no V instructions; the
 *          QEMU plugin's per-insn callback misreports the drained PC when
 *          the immediately-preceding code uses V).
 *   vsim:  vle32.v v8..v15 from the seed; vadd.vi v9 += 100; vse8.v
 *          v0..v31 to the V-scratch region.
 *   QEMU2: vle8.v v0..v31 from V-scratch (round-trip the regfile back into
 *          QEMU's V state), then vse32.v v8..v15 + verify; v8/v10..v15
 *          unchanged from seed, v9 == seed + 100.
 *
 * V-state transport
 *   The vsim hart's V regfile is not exposed via Debug Module Abstract
 *   Commands (the spec leaves V to optional Program Buffer / DPI). As a
 *   stop-gap we use a fixed scratch region inside the shared mmap (V_SCRATCH
 *   below, matching hybrid::kVScratchGpa). vsim's StateDrain reads those
 *   bytes into hybrid_state_v1::vreg[] so qemu_handback can ship them to
 *   QEMU 2 via existing gdbstub P packets. ResumeDriver does the inverse on
 *   the way in, but this fixture's QEMU1 phase doesn't use V so the
 *   inbound V state is all zeros -- vsim re-seeds v0..v31 itself by
 *   loading from the SEED region.
 *
 * Memory layout:
 *   SEED at 0x80100000        - input pattern (16 int32, 64 bytes)
 *   PHASE_C_OUT at 0x80102000 - QEMU2 verify scratch (8 regs * 64 = 512 B)
 *   V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000   - matches hybrid::kVScratchGpa (32 V regs *
 *                                64 bytes = 2048 B)
 *   stack at 0x80700000       - rt_c.ld places _stack here
 *
 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512 (vlenb=64). QEMU launched with
 * -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512; vsim built from
 * data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <stdint.h>

#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */
#define VLENB  64  /* bytes per V reg */
#define NREGS  8   /* test v8..v15 */

static int32_t* const SEED        = (int32_t*) 0x80100000UL;
static int32_t* const PHASE_C_OUT = (int32_t*) 0x80102000UL;

#define V_SCRATCH 0x80FF0000UL

static void seed_pattern(int32_t* dst, int n)
{
  for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
    dst[i] = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5);
}

int main(void)
{
  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed memory only. No V instructions here -- the
   * plugin's gdbstub-pc readback misreports the drain PC when V is in
   * flight, which would resume vsim at the wrong instruction. */
  seed_pattern(SEED, VL_E32);

  rt_phase_qemu1_drain();

  /* Phase 2 (vsim): set mstatus.VS, load v8..v15 from SEED, mutate v9,
   * then save the full v0..v31 regfile to V_SCRATCH so vsim's drain can
   * read them out for the QEMU 2 hop.
   *
   * mstatus.VS=Initial via csrs is necessary because vsim's DM does not
   * honor abstract-write of mstatus on resume. */
  asm volatile(
    "li     t1, (1 << 9) | (1 << 13)\n"   /* VS=Initial | FS=Initial */
    "csrs   mstatus, t1\n"
    /* Reload v8..v15 from SEED via 32-bit unit-stride loads. */
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v8,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v9,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v10, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v11, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v12, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v13, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v14, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    /* Mutate v9 by +100 (vadd.vi takes 5-bit signed imm; +10 ten times). */
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    /* Save v0..v31 to V_SCRATCH for vsim's drain (e8 stride to write
     * exactly vlenb bytes per reg, regardless of element semantics). */
    "li     a4, 64\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "li     t0, 0x80FF0000\n"
    "vse8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory","t0","t1","a4");

  rt_phase_vsim_drain();

  /* Phase 3 (QEMU2): the gdbstub P packets (sent by qemu_handback) have
   * already populated v0..v31 in QEMU's V state from state.vreg[]. Run
   * vse32.v v8..v15 to PHASE_C_OUT and verify the bytes match the seed
   * pattern (with v9 +100). */
  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "addi t0, %0, 0\n"
    "vse32.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(PHASE_C_OUT)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  for (int reg = 0; reg < NREGS; reg++) {
    int32_t* row = PHASE_C_OUT + reg * VL_E32;
    int32_t bias = (reg == 1) ? 100 : 0;  /* v9 is reg index 1 in v8..v15 */
    for (int i = 0; i < VL_E32; i++) {
      int32_t expected = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5) + bias;
      if (row[i] != expected) {
        rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: FAIL\n");
        return 1;
      }
    }
  }
  rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: PASS\n");
  return 0;
}

    "vse8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory","t0","t1","a4");

  rt_phase_vsim_drain();

  /* Phase 3 (QEMU2): the gdbstub P packets (sent by qemu_handback) have
   * already populated v0..v31 in QEMU's V state from state.vreg[]. Run
   * vse32.v v8..v15 to PHASE_C_OUT and verify the bytes match the seed
   * pattern (with v9 +100).
   *
   * mstatus.VS may not survive the gdbstub mstatus P packet round-trip,
   * so set it again here. */
  asm volatile(
    "li     t1, (1 << 9) | (1 << 13)\n"
    "csrs   mstatus, t1\n"
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "addi t0, %0, 0\n"
    "vse32.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(PHASE_C_OUT)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  for (int reg = 0; reg < NREGS; reg++) {
    int32_t* row = PHASE_C_OUT + reg * VL_E32;
    int32_t bias = (reg == 1) ? 100 : 0;  /* v9 is reg index 1 in v8..v15 */
    for (int i = 0; i < VL_E32; i++) {
      int32_t expected = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5) + bias;
      if (row[i] != expected) {
        rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: FAIL\n");
        return 1;
      }
    }
  }
  rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: PASS\n");
  return 0;
}
